title: Dungeons:Walda
text:
Instructions:
Expand on Walda's role in the prequel novel, "The Rise of The Arch-Illager."
Gender: Female
Species: Illager
Status: Unknown
Affiliations: Illager ( Evoker )
Residence: Woodland Mansion Highblock Halls
Allies: Arch-Illager Thord ​ [ formerly ]
Enemies: Salah Yumi
First appearance: Minecraft Dungeons
Latest appearance: The Rise of the Arch-Illager
Actor: Dan Bittner (audiobook)
Walda is a female evoker who used to be the leader of Archie 's tribe until Archie stumbled upon the Orb of Dominance which granted him immense power, dropping her place as leader of the tribe. She appears in Minecraft Dungeons:The Rise of the Arch-Illager and she was the one who crowned the Arch-Illager in the opening sequence of the game.
Appearance
Walda is a tall and skinny evoker with typical illager characteristics, such as gray skin and thick eyebrows. She uses feminine pronouns. As seen in her only official appearance in the game's opening cinematic, she wears an evoker's signature black robe with yellow trim. The leaders of the Illager tribe had never worn any special clothes or had anything that marked them as exceptional before Archie's rise to power, implying she, in fact, has a physical appearance similar to that of any evoker.
Personality
Having been the leader of a tribe of Illagers for a long time, Walda perpetrates the standards and notions of her people, such as their beliefs in the usage of strength and cunning to achieve power over others and to keep it. She has obtained a unique set of knowledge and wisdom from her experiences as a tribe chief and as an evoker, which she is not afraid to use in her favor. In her younger years, she led nomadic Illagers through much of the Mainland, granting her a lot of familiarity with the region. She has been reported to be the best Illager leader in the land, but even then, her wildest ambitions have only involved keeping her people together and fed. She believes that the true might of her Illagers comes from them staying together; otherwise, they stand no chance against their threats.
She exhibits many traits associated with good leadership, such as her assertiveness and unyielding behavior. She has enough practice in rulership to be able to assess her environment appropriately and quickly, being ready to make a decision even in desperate situations. She does not think rulership works particularly well by defeating all who challenge your authority, as that can give a reason for others to think that they might have been right. The penalty for stealing from fellow Illagers in her tribe is severe, with Walda meting out justice without mercy. Her pride has made her reluctant to bow down to anybody, but she will do so if she has to. After having lost control over the tribe to Archie, she connived at the theft of resources from the Fiery Forge by Thord, hoping he could defeat Archie, showcasing her power-hunger and willingness to conspire against others to reclaim power. She begins to fear Archie and constantly tries to appeal to his vanity to save herself as he starts to threaten her life and destroys Thord at last.
Biography
Thord and Archie eventually arrive at the woodland mansion, where they are greeted by the other Illagers. Somehow, Archie has an idea, running past Thord and greeting Walda, the leader of the Illagers. Archie then explains to Walda that the hero had stole the victory from the Illagers, putting the blame on Thord by saying that it was him that thought he could take on a hero all by himself. The other Illagers gasp in response. Archie then asks Walda if she had appointed Thord herself, in which she replies that she did. She then says that the Illagers still haven’t learned the lesson from the last time they died, saying that they still charge into battle without thinking about other dangers. Thord and Archie then have an argument about who was in charge of their particular group, specifically as to who was at fault for the death of the raiding party. Archie then punches Thord, who drops to the ground, clutching his injured leg. Archie yells at him to get up, kicking him in the thigh. Walda then says to Archie that because of his actions, he would be banished from the group of Illagers, seemingly forever.
Later, after Archie has found the Orb of Dominance and Highblock Keep, he chances upon Thord and his Illager raising party. He demands that Thord inform Walda of his rise to power and convince her to visit the Keep. Thord presumably executed those tasks, as Walda shortly shows up at Highblock Keep.
According to the Nintendo Switch News' Minecraft Dungeons channel article, Walda was the Evoker that was defeated by the heroes at the end of the Pumpkin Pastures mission. [ citation needed ]
Quotes
“ It horrifies me that you would accuse each other of such terrible things. Illagers must stick together, even on the field of battle. Especially on the field of battle. Otherwise, we stand no chance against our foes. Do you remember what it was like when we roamed the lands, each to ourselves? The mobs picked us off one by one, destroying us at their will. It was only when we united—when we became a tribe— that we stood any chance against them at all. „ — Walda
Gallery
Walda crowning Archie as the new leader of the Illagers.
Portrait of Walda.
Trivia
The name Walda is a modern anglicization of the Old English name wealda, which means "ruler".
Besides Archie, Walda is the only non- hero character from the novel to get an official appearance, although she does not look much different from regular evokers.

title: Dungeons:Broken Citadel
text:
This article is about the mission. For the tower floor, see MCD:Broken Citadel Combat Floor.
This page describes content that is a part of the Echoing Void DLC.
Part of: Other Dimensions
Boss: Vengeful Heart of Ender
Minimum difficulty: IV
Unlock predecessor: End Wilds
Raid captains: Can have captains but never all six
Daily trials: Can have daily trials
Time to End This! You've reached the very heart of the Void and found the last bastion of the enemy's power. It's time to end the tale of the Orb of Dominance and put a stop to this chaos.
Broken Citadel is the sixth other dimensions mission in Minecraft Dungeons, is part of the Echoing Void DLC, and is the final mission of the DLC story. It is unlocked after the completion of End Wilds, and completing Broken Citadel does not unlock any missions. Broken Citadel is counted as a boss mission for Apocalypse Plus progression, due to it containing the Vengeful Heart of Ender.

title: Dungeons:???
text:
Not to be confused with MCD:Moo Room  or  MCD:?????.
"???" redirects here. For the element, see Element § ???.
Part of: Mainland
Boss: Mooshroom Monstrosity
Minimum difficulty: IV
Unlock predecessor: Moo Room scroll
Raid captains: Always has six captains
Daily trials: Cannot have daily trials
Moo Moo? Moo!
??? or Moo is the sixth mainland secret mission in Minecraft Dungeons. It is unlocked after collecting the scroll found in Moo Room, a sub dungeon in Camp accessed by collecting all nine runes from the various rune rooms. Completing ??? does not unlock any missions. ??? is counted as a boss mission for Apocalypse Plus progression, due to it containing the Mooshroom Monstrosity.
Appearance and description
“ SECRET MISSION – At the top left corner of your map, you will see an outcrop of islands labeled "???." You will find yourself transported to a land filled with giant mushrooms and populated by highly aggressive mooshrooms. How these usually gentle beasts came to be so angry is a mystery, but what is clear is that they can deal serious damage to heroes who enter their territory. Fight your way through the hordes of herds and you will be rewarded with valuable treasure. „ — Guide to Minecraft Dungeons:
A Handbook for Heroes
??? is an illuminated and isolated mushroom fields island populated by abnormally aggressive mooshrooms. It contains fields and chasms with giant red mushrooms and waterfalls. It features small red mushrooms, pop flowers, glowing mushrooms, wooden bridges, spring platforms, and a single small wooden cottage. The mission starts next to a small wooden dock and boat, used by the heroes to travel to the isolated island. Halfway through the mission, a small wooden cottage with red mushrooms growing on its roof can be found. Near the end of the mission, a spring platform must be used to launch into an arena containing the Mooshroom Monstrosity. Defeating the Mooshroom Monstrosity will lower the gate to the north-east, granting access to another small wooden boat that can be used to exit the mission.
Unlocking
Main article: MCD:Great Hall § Unlocking ???
The entrance door of Moo Room, the sub dungeon where ???'s scroll is found, is located in Camp at the back of Great Hall.

Trivia
???'s name went through several iterations during development, including Mooshroom Island, Isle of Moo, and Mycelia.
It is the only mission and the only secret mission to not contain any ambushes.
???'s music track is made up of cow moos, and is played in reverse during the second half of the music track.
The Mooshroom Monstrosity is not a fungi-covered Redstone Monstrosity previously defeated in Fiery Forge. This is because the Redstone Monstrosity from Fiery Forge had its head mounted on the wall in Home.

title: Dungeons:Karl
text:
"Karl" redirects here. For the Mojang Studios employee, see Karl Lowry. Gender: Male
Species: Hero
Status: Unknown
Aliases: Ender of Illagers Smacker
Affiliations: Heroes
Enemies: Arch-Illager
First appearance: Minecraft Dungeons
Latest appearance: Rise of the Arch-Illager
Actor: Dan Bittner (audiobook)
Karl is a hero from the novel The Rise of the Arch-Illager. He is an arrogant member of the main group of heroes, going after anyone and anything that stands in his way. This leads to him becoming the center of the Orb of Dominance 's anger and the main antagonist of the novel.
Appearance

Karl has ginger hair and wears chainmail that resembles pillager armour. However, it has a different palette, being lead grey and grey, as opposed to leathery brown and a slightly lighter brown. He also wields a "jagged" iron sword.
Personality
Karl is shown to be a rash and headstrong Hero, depicted as a stereotypical snob who will kill anything that is a hindrance to him, and looks down on those he considers beneath him. He seems to love challenges, happily taking on two redstone golems in the book when Archie initially invaded Squid Coast, believing his sword makes him practically invincible against the golems.
Biography
Spoiler warning! This section contains detailed information about this book that may spoil your enjoyment of it. Read at your own risk!
In the prologue of the novel, Karl notices a horde of hostile mobs attacking a group of Illagers outside a village. He decides to attack the hostile mobs with his iron sword, killing them off with ease. The Illagers take notice of Karl and plan to attack him, but one of them, described as "a particularly short one with a massive nose" begins to speak. Although Karl couldn’t understand what the Illager was saying, as they spoke two completely different languages, he let him speak out of curiosity, soon understanding that the Illager was practically begging the others to spare Karl, seeing as he had killed the hostile mobs that had attacked them. Another Illager, an evoker, then smacks the begging Illager on the side of its head with its sword, ending its blabbering. Karl then kills off the rest of the Illagers with relative ease, except for the short one with the massive nose. Karl explains to this particular Illager that he’s not going to hurt him and lets him go back to his home, making sure he tells the other Illagers the name: Karl. After Karl began to head back towards the village, the Illager "seethed in frustration and fury."
Later on in chapters five and six of the novel, Karl, along with four other heroes, arrive in a village on the Squid Coast with the intent of trading with the villagers. Karl sees an Illager trying to run from them. He grabs him and recognizes him as the short Illager whose life he spared. Karl immediately releases him from his grasp and watches him collapse onto the ground. One of the four heroes with Karl pulls out a potion of healing offers it to the Illager. However, before the Illager can reach for it, Karl smacks the hero’s hand away. The heroes then have a brief argument about the Illager, while two of the villagers also get into an argument about the Illager as well. Karl appears to laugh at the two villagers for arguing, which only makes one of them enraged. Just as he opens his mouth, the Illager punches him in the crotch. The villager falls to his knees, groaning in pain. The Illager then kicks the same villager again, making everyone gasp in horror. Karl then backhands Archie across his cheek, sprawling him into some grass.
After that, Karl then draws his sword in an attempt to kill the Illager. Just as he is about to stab the Illager, the other villager charges in-between them, causing her and Karl to fall onto the ground. Karl springs to his feet. The villager tries to stand up, but Karl kicks her feet out from underneath, making the other heroes feel stunned by his actions. The first villager then tells Karl to leave the other villager alone, but he is also thrown back by Karl’s sword, crumpling next to the Illager and falling unconscious. The other heroes begin protesting Karl’s actions. The Illager, feeling fearful that he might attack the villager, makes his move. He punches Karl in the stomach, which causes his arms to rise, revealing a thin strip of flesh underneath his armor shirt. Without hesitation, the Illager then punches Karl in the gut. Karl immediately yelps in pain, dropping his sword, and clutching his stomach with his hands. He then tries to attack the Illager, but two of his fellow heroes stop him from doing so. With Karl distracted, the Illager attempts to escape with one of the villagers, but when that fails, the Illager tries to hide in the villager’s house. The villager tries defending him by preventing from Karl and the heroes from entering the house. When Karl tries to attack the villager, the other four heroes intervene and pull him away from the villager. Although they try to reason with Karl, he refuses to listen to them. Eventually, each of the four heroes grab Karl by a limb and literally carry him out of the village. As they leave, the Illager pumps his fist into the air and cheers, but the other villagers appear with torches and pitchforks.
In the first interlude, the group of heroes arrive at a hilltop overlooking the village. One of the heroes asks Karl if he was out of his mind with attacking the villagers. Karl responds with a simple "yeah", and feels like that other heroes are people who competed with him for resources. It is then revealed that Karl made up nicknames for the other heroes because he couldn’t be bothered to remember their real names: Stache, Red, Scarface, and Pinky. The five embroil in a long argument about villages and villagers, with the other four shooting down every argument that Karl makes. Eventually, cooler heads prevail and Karl begins setting up camp on the hilltop, although he still thinks that the villagers might attack them because of his actions. Not long after, Adriene, taking watch on the edge of the hilltop, notices someone heading eastward. The other heroes join her and are surprised when it’s found out to be the Illager from the village, who is heading toward the Soggy Swamp. Karl voices his intention to attack him, but Hal and Hex intervene, saying that it isn’t worth his time to do so. The three then realize that the Illager had been kicked out of the village, with Karl feeling relieved, saying that at least he won’t be able to attack anyone else.

Karl in the opening of Minecraft Dungeons.

title: Dungeons:Hex
text:
Obtaining: Automatically obtained
Level ID: Hex
Species: Hero
Status: Alive
Aliases: Scarface
Pronouns: They/Them
Allies: Adriene Hal Valorie
First appearance: Minecraft Dungeons
Latest appearance: Minecraft
Actor: Dan Bittner (audiobook)
Hex, known as Scarface to Karl, is one of the four main heroes within Minecraft Dungeons, along with Valorie, Hal, and Adriene.
Appearance

Hex is a pale-skinned figure concealed in armor, primarily colored navy blue and brown. Their only exposed skin is their face, with visible brown hair and scars.
Personality

Biography

Hex appears in Minecraft Dungeons: The Rise of the Arch-Illager, intervening when Karl attempts to attack Archie.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Sept 29, 2018, Hex appears in theMinecraft Dungeonsannouncement trailer.
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added Hex.

Trivia
Hex appears as a playable character in Minecraft: Builders & Biomes.
On the book cover for Minecraft Dungeons: The Rise of the Arch-Illager, Hex is seen equipped with a pair of daggers. In the Official Launch Trailer, Hex is seen equipped with sickles and Dark Armor.

title: Dungeons:Corrupted Cauldron
text:
For other uses, see Cauldron (disambiguation).
Health points: 7000
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Enemies
Mob category: Boss
Attack strength: Poison cloud: 75 Sorrow (death): 1000 base damage to hostile mob
Spawn: Soggy Swamp ( boss ) Daily Trial Tower ( boss )
The Corrupted Cauldron, or simply The Cauldron, is the first boss of the game. It is encountered at the end of Soggy Swamp. The boss attacks by generating a ring fire around itself and by summoning enemies such as the conjured slime.
Spawning
Soggy Swamp ( boss )
Daily Trial
Tower ( boss )
Drops
Main article: MCD:Drops § Soggy Swamp
Armor: Evocation RobeEmber RobeVerdant RobeMystery Armor, Artifacts: Fishing RodHarvesterLightning RodTotem of Regeneration, Melee weapons: DaggersFangs of FrostMoon DaggersSheer DaggersDouble AxeCursed AxeWhirlwindGlaiveGrave BaneVenom GlaiveSoul KnifeEternal KnifeTruthseeker, Ranged weapons: Hunting BowAncient BowHunter's PromiseMaster's BowScatter CrossbowLightning Harp CrossbowHarp Crossbow
Behavior
The Corrupted Cauldron has three behaviors. It does not move during its battle, even if struck by a Gravity -enchanted weapon.
Basic attack
The Corrupted Cauldron creates a ring of purple fire on the mossy cobblestone blocks around it, which deals heavy damage to anything that steps in it. Used occasionally.
Intense spin
The Corrupted Cauldron, after a stirring animation, conjures a variety of dangerous mobs to join the battle.
Strong attack
The Corrupted Cauldron sucks up the corpses of any minion that is not a conjured slime. Once the corpse of a mob is inside of the Corrupted Cauldron, the cauldron consumes it, regenerating 1% of its total health. It is somewhat similar to the leeching enchantment.
Minions
These minions are summoned by the Corrupted Cauldron. Minions are related with their own summoner.
The Corrupted Cauldron can only absorb its own minions' corpses except conjured slimes. For example, in the tower, the Corrupted Cauldron does not suck up drowned corpses (summoned by drowned necromancer ). Minions within 20 blocks are killed by the Corrupted Cauldron if it is defeated.

title: Dungeons:Smiting
text:
Not to be confused with Smite  or  Smithing.
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Melee Weapon Ranged Weapon
Soul gear only: No
In-game description Increases damage against the Undead.
Smiting is an enchantment that can be found within the various melee and ranged weapons of Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: +25% increased damage, Cost: 1 Enchantment Point
Tier: II, Effect: +35% increased damage, Cost: 2 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: +45% increased damage, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Usage
Smiting increases the damage that is dealt to undead mobs. The damage boost increases by 10% with each tier, at the maximum tier the damage increase is +45%.
Undead enemies
Mobs that take extra damage from smiting include:
Baby Drowned
Baby Zombie
Chicken Jockey
Chicken Jockey Tower
Drowned
Drowned Necromancer
Frozen Zombie
Husk
Jungle Zombie
Mossy Skeleton
Nameless One
Necromancer
Skeleton
Skeleton Horseman
Skeleton Vanguard
Stray
Sunken Skeleton
Trident Drowned
Wretched Wraith
Wither Skeleton
Wither Skeleton Archer
Wraith
Zombie
Zombified Fungus Thrower
Zombified Piglin
Zombified Piglin Hunter
Stacking
Smiting is stackable, meaning it can appear in multiple enchantment slots on an item, although there are exceptions. If Smiting is the built-in unique enchantment of an item it will never be able to appear in the gilded slot or other enchantment slots by any means. If Smiting appears in the gilded slot of an item it will never appear in the other three enchantment slots, however by rerolling the item's enchantments using the enchantsmith, Smiting has the chance to appear even if it appears in the gilded slot.
When stacked it has a multiplicative effect, meaning each extra Smiting will have its undead mobs damage increase multiplied by the last.
Built-into
Smiting tier I is built-into the following weapons:
Dark Katana
Cackling Broom
Grave Bane

title: Legends:Great Bungus
text:
Species: Piglin ​ [ formerly ] Zombified Piglin
Status: Alive (zombified)
Aliases: Fungus
Occupation: Bastion leader ​ [ formerly ]
Affiliations: Bastion
Allies: Uggub Kritten ​ [ formerly ]
Enemies: Grungert Kritten Farnum
First appearance: Minecraft Legends: Return of the Piglins
Actor: Sean Patrick Hopkins (audiobook)
The Great Bungus, also known as the Powerful Bungus, and Bungus, was the leader of the largest piglin clan within the Nether of Minecraft Legends: Return of the Piglins. Their name was mistaken as Great Fungus and Fungus by Farnum due to the language barrier.
Appearance

The possible appearance of The Great Bungus is hinted on the cover of the book. It is likely that they looked like a portal guard from Minecraft Legends wearing the horns and crown of the Great Hog.
Personality

Biography
Originally, Bungus was just another piglin brute in the litter, described by Kritten as a brutish bully. However, using Kritten’s aid they rose up the ranks of a large bastion led by the Great Grungert, before declaring themselves the new ruler. Initially they were said to be quite grateful and generous to Kritten, valuing their insight like no one else, but over time let the control get to them. By the time we meet them, Kritten was just another face in a room to Bungus, treating them with regular verbal abuse and negligence.
Quotes
“ As the leader of the largest—and arguably the most powerful—clan of piglins in the entire Nether, Bungus liked to make demands and to have them answered instantly. They hadn’t fought their way up to the top of this heap to have people ignore their demands, no matter how ridiculous they might be. „
Gallery
A piglin resembling the in-book descriptions of Bungus on the cover.

Appearance
The Ancient Hoglin is considerably larger than typical hoglins, though possessing a similar shape. It has strikemaster-colored skin that is covered in sores, similar to warboars. Its back hairs are much longer and thicker, resembles blades of grass in shape, with visible markings on the face. Most notable of all is their tusks, which are much more numerous and growing in odd places, including on the nose.
Biography
The Ancient Hoglin could be seen being chased by The Beast during multiple cutscenes alongside several smaller, normal hoglins. It managed to escape from The Beast due to it being forced to go through a portal by The Seer.

The Well of Fate dimension is the area in which the Hosts ( Knowledge, Action, and Foresight ) appear to reside.
Geography
The dimension appears to be made up of water and a number of unknown blue colored blocks, some of which resemble prismarine or prismarine bricks. The sky is a dark teal in color, with what appear to be stars in the distance.
Only two locations in the dimension are shown: the room or structure where the Hero first meets the Hosts, and an outside training area that contains a few trees and stone structures from the Overworld.
Properties
During cutscenes, entities from the Overworld turn blue and translucent while inside of the dimension, while the inverse is true for the Hosts. This does not appear to apply for organic matter in general, as flowers and trees appear unchanged.

title: Legends:The Seer
text:
This article is about the villain in Minecraft Legends. For the adaptation in A Minecraft Movie, see Movie:Malgosha.
Details
Species: Piglin
Status: Unknown
Affiliations: The Great Hog
Residence: Nether
First appearance: Minecraft Legends
Actor: Cristina Vee
The Seer is an elderly piglin that can be found within the various cutscenes of Minecraft Legends.
Biography
Spoiler warning! This section contains detailed information about Minecraft Legends that may spoil your enjoyment of it. Read at your own risk!
The Seer possesses the Orb of dominance which they use when called upon by the Great Hog. They use it to turn a beacon into the Night Beacon. Later, The Seer senses when the Night Beacon is destroyed by the Hero and their allies.
As the Hero keeps destroying Horde of the Hunt portals, a messenger piglin that The Beast ignored informs the Seer, who listens to it. The Seer then orders the Beast to go to the Overworld. As The Beast seems to have no interest, the Seer starts to get more impatient. In the aftermath, The Seer uses a large shockwave in order to make The Beast go through a nether portal to the Overworld, which doesn't work. Only when the Seer stops The Beast using their telekinetic powers does The Beast go through the portal.
After the Great Hog learns of his generals’ deaths and angrily hits away the messengers who told him, the Seer uses the Orb of Dominance to enhance their leader’s staff. They then watch as he smashes a Well of Fate replica and subsequently invades the Overworld himself.
The Seer remains in the Nether even after the Great Hog is defeated, and thus their fate is currently unknown.
Powers and abilities
The Seer is shown to be incredibly powerful, possessing the Orb of Dominance and creating the Night Beacon. Among their capabilities, they can summon a purple shockwave of light and utilize telekinesis.

Minecraft Legends: TU0, 1.17.24827, Minecraft Legendsis released, featuring the Seer in cutscenes.

title: Legends:Messenger Piglin
text:
Details
Species: Piglin
Status: Deceased (presumed)
Affiliations: The Beast The Devourer The Great Hog The Unbreakable
Residence: Nether
First appearance: Minecraft Legends
A messenger piglin, also known as a grunter, was a type of piglin that relayed information to the piglin horde generals.
Appearance
Messenger piglins generally look like piglin runts, making them about the same size as modern day piglins. Their noses have asymmetrical nostrils, as are their eyes, one slightly higher than the other. Their ears on the other hand are perfectly symmetrical, with shorts with two large, gold buttons.
The injured piglin is a messenger piglin who is very visibly injured, their head predominately covered in bandages, down to the tip of their ears. One of their eyes is obscured, while their nose is slanted to the right. Their limbs are almost all covered in bandages as well, with visible scars.
Biography
During the war many messenger piglins were sent to give information to the various leaders of the hordes, after the Hero and allied mobs managed to thwart their various schemes. One such messenger piglin was injured after the hero destroyed a nether portal in a piglin base belonging to the Horde of the Spore. This messenger piglin went back into the nether with bandages and tried to get The Devourer 's attention to tell them that another nether portal had been destroyed, but the piglin was not successful. The Devourer charged into the portal the injured piglin was near, entering into it, though one of the Devourer's pink spores fell onto the injured piglin's back, causing the piglin to try to eat it.
History
Minecraft Legends: TU0, 1.17.24827, Minecraft Legendsis released, featuring messenger piglins and the injured piglin.

text:

A protector tower is a structure that destroys nearby large enemy projectilles within Minecraft Legends.
Obtaining
Protector towers can be placed by using a build allay, which will cost:
Campaign: 75 wood, 75 stone and 60 coal.
Versus Mode: 450 stone and 200 iron.
Usage
Protector towers are player anti-siege buildings that destroy large enemy projectiles that come within their small radius, while smaller projectiles will be ignored. In the Campaign, they destroy most large piglin projectiles. In Versus Mode, they additionally destroy enemy players' large projectiles such as redstone launcher projectiles. They can only be upgraded by a masonry.

title: Legends:Stun Tower
text:
Type: Power Tower
Cost: Campaign: 125 Stone 100 Coal 1 Stun Tower Core Versus Mode: 600 Stone 400 Coal
Improvement needed: Build Power Towers
Identifier: badger:entity_struct_poi_tower_04
A stun tower is a defensive power tower that stuns enemies within Minecraft Legends.
Lore
Knowledge was responsible for the construction of power towers during the Overworld's creation to assist the Hosts. Villagers were involved in the building of stun towers, which were built as a home for musical allays. However, when the Hero discovered a stun tower, the Hosts notice that the bell in one of the towers rung loudly at a group of piglin runts and stunned them.
Spawning
Campaign
Stun towers can be found naturally already built in badlands and swamp biomes, though they won’t work or be able to be used until collected.
Obtaining
Campaign
Stun towers can be built for 125 stone, 100 coal, and one stun tower core using a build allay. To obtain stun tower cores, a stun tower must be found within the overworld and 100 gold and 25 coal must be spent when interacting with one. To do this, the collect power towers improvement must have been previously built at an improvement hub.
Versus
Stun towers can be built by players for 600 stone and 400 coal using a build allay. To do this, the build power towers improvement must have been previously built at an improvement hub.
Usage
Stun towers emit a sonic attack that stuns any nearby enemies, but not damage them. It cannot be buffed by other structures, but can be repaired by Carpenter Hut.
Quotes
Journal
Name: Stun Tower, Category: Overworld, Unlocked by: Campaign:Collecting a stun towerVersus Mode:Building Improvement Gather Coal, Quote: "When rung, the stun tower's bell emits a sound that stops the piglins in their tracks. To collect these mysterious towers, you must build a special improvement. Those chimes sure sound lovely... Makes you wonder what their original purpose was."
Player Building Melodies
Name: Stun Tower, Category: Player Building Melodies, Unlocked by: Campaign:Collecting a stun towerVersus Mode:Building Improvement Gather Coal, Quote: "Emits a sonic attack that will stun any enemy within range. Cannot damage enemies. Cannot be buffed by other structures. Ancient host tower. Rings loudly when enemies approach and stuns them in place. Does not damage enemies, so it needs support."
Other
“ Stuns piglins in a very large area. A Host relic that was once home to the musical allays. „ — Stun tower map legend

Minecraft Earth

Mobs

Health
The player can eat food to regain health in Adventures.
Health is now persistent between Adventures. If the player takes damage in one Adventure, they will be hurt when they start their next Adventure.
Health is displayed while the player is on the Map. If the player dies in an adventure, they respawn on the map but with reduced health.

Bucket of Mud
Stone Slab
Sandstone
Spruce Button
General
Inventory
Item Info button on the Inventory Screen – the player can click through to see item stats and discover how chicken compares to mutton, or what the damage and durability values for their tools are.
Settings

Other
Players no longer get stuck in the Adventure preview screen.

text:

Player journal description The furnace golem is a fierce mob, smashing its enemies into the air and scorching the earth with its fiery attacks. Don't be scared though! It will protect you from hostile mobs that lurk in the darkness.
A furnace golem was an iron golem variant with a blast furnace as its body, found in Minecraft Earth. Furnace golems were also known as iron furnace golems.
Behavior
When a furnace golem saw any hostile mob, its face and eyes ignited before attacking, which had a chance of setting the hostile mob on fire. A furnace golem didn't stop attacking until the hostile mob was killed. Once killed, the golem's eyes and face reverted to gray. During the attacks, a furnace golem was also able to light its surroundings on fire, causing blocks to burn and spread flames, which threatened builds.
Furnace golems handed out torches, similar to iron golems giving poppies to baby villagers. This behavior was triggered randomly by viewing the golem in a certain angle.
Naturally spawned furnace golems found in adventures were neutral mobs that attacked the player if provoked by being hit.
Furnace golems were also damaged by water.

Tappables

title: Story Mode:Jailhouse Block
text:
Verify the plot details if they are interpreted correctly from the game.
Details
Episode: 3
Season: 2
Release date: September 19, 2017
Written by: Adam Douglas
Directed by: Christopher Rieser
Jailhouse Block, also known as Episode 3, is the third episode of Telltale Games ' Minecraft: Story Mode - Season Two. It was released on September 19, 2017. Jesse is imprisoned in the Admin’s high-security Sunshine Institute and must plan an escape while forming alliances with fellow inmates.
Synopsis
“ Jesse and the gang must brave a dangerous prison and its even more dangerous inmates to reach the secret at the prison's heart... But when the enemy tries to recruit Jesse's friends, will they be able to resist the call? „ — In-game description.
Plot
Jesse gets teleported to an area filled with obsidian and lava, with a speakerphone telling Jesse to follow an "orange line" to a "check-in zone". Jesse does so, and the line takes them to a place known as the Sunshine Institute. The warden greets Jesse and explains to them that the institute is actually a reformatory for "difficult people". Petra / Jack also show up and attack both the warden and Big Hank, allowing Jesse some time to escape. The two then go on a footrace with several associates and Big Hanks in an attempt to reach "The Burrows", but are soon caught by a Big Hank. The warden then sends Petra / Jack to the "Mush Room", and sends Jesse to his office.
In the warden's office, the warden introduces Jesse to Radar and Nurm, and also puts Radar under "The Iron Breathtaker", and the player can choose whether or not to work for the warden as an associate. Stella also shows up and informs the warden to watch Jesse for "trouble", mentioning their "rivalry" once more. The three (Jesse, Stella, and Radar) then head to the "Mush Room", which is basically concrete cells with a "courtyard". Inside, they meet Anthony (Bigbst4tz2), Jesse chats with numerous "guests", and is able to give Radar a cookie / mushroom stew. Suddenly, Prisoner X (revealed to be named Xara) escapes, and Anthony informs the four (Jesse, Radar, Petra / Jack, and Nurm) to go to a cell for safety. Petra / Jack explain to Jesse that Xara is locked in a cell deep underground, and Jesse believes that Xara and them need to work together.
Back in the "courtyard", Jesse believes that they can get a promotion if they find if Oxblood is doing something against the rules, and is able to find Oxblood keeping a mooshroom named Geoff in captivity. The warden then explains to Jesse that they have to shear the mooshroom in order to get promoted. Depending on if you choose to shear the mooshroom or not, Jesse (by themself if you shear it) and the four (only if you don't shear it) get taken to the Zombie Mines, an area where "guests" harvest zombies for rotten flesh. Jesse discovers that there is a redstone circuit connected to Xara's cell, and concludes that a lever needs to be made in order to bypass it. Jesse can make a truce with Stella if the player decides to do so, and is able to bypass the circuit to Xara's door. Just as Jesse is about to enter Xara's cell, the Admin shows up, punishes the warden, and orders Jesse to fight Petra / Jack. The battle ends in a draw after the two team up to go against the Admin. Suddenly, the Admin disguises himself as Jesse, and teleports Stella to Beacontown with him. The five (Jesse, Petra, Jack, Radar and Nurm) then hunt down for Xara.
At Xara's cell, it is revealed that the Admin's real name is Romeo, and Xara agrees to help Jesse escape on the condition that one of them (Nurm or Lluna) stay behind (this is so the explosives don't get triggered), and the prisoners escape the Sunshine Institute. In the burrows, the warden is in a rage over Prisoner X's escape and reveals that Xara was a former Admin. Just as the warden is about to ignite TNT, Xara kills him with a poison arrow, and the six (Jesse, Petra, Radar, Jack, Xara and Nurm / Lluna) fight numerous creatures, including creeper-spiders, known as creeders and the three-headed ghast. Xara then reveals that there was a third Admin named Fred, who died during the battle with Xara against Romeo. Xara also reveals that the only way to take away Romeo's powers is to go below the bedrock.
In a post-credits scene, Romeo (disguised as Jesse), steals the Order's amulet and summons a guard to track them down.

title: Story Mode:Below the Bedrock
text:
Verify the plot details if they are interpreted correctly from the game.
Details
Episode: 4
Season: 2
Release date: November 7, 2017
Written by: Nicole Martinez, Meredith Ainsworth, and Doug Lieblich
Directed by: Daniel Rosales
Below the Bedrock, also known as Episode 4, is the fourth episode of Telltale Games ' Minecraft: Story Mode - Season Two. It was released on November 7, 2017. Jesse's group discover a forgotten world beneath bedrock, where they uncover secrets about the Admin’s past and search for a way to stop his rule.
Summary
“ Our heroes take a journey to a long-forgotten land where nothing is quite what it seems and danger lurks around every corner. Will Jesse's new alliances and friendships withstand the tests they'll face? „ — In-game description.
Plot
In Below the Bedrock, the six (Jesse, Xara, Petra, Radar, Jack and Nurm/Lluna) make their way down a tower. Suddenly, a giant enderman appears and begins dismantling the tower, but the group barely escapes and make their way toward the Oasis, a city that supposedly has an automated farm. At the Oasis, it is revealed that the city is in ruins. Xara gets angry over a destroyed bed, but eventually gets over it, and tells Jesse that the portal is the only way home, and that the secret weapon is in Fred's house at Fred's Keep. The group (Jesse, Petra, Radar, Jack and Nurm/Lluna) then go on a minecart track toward Fred's Keep.
On the way to Fred's Keep, the giant enderman takes out a portion of the track, causing the group to separate. Jesse and Jack are hanging on the edge of a cliff, and are cornered by people who "sniff out for outsiders" (one of whom is named PorkChop). They offer their assistance in exchange for their swords. Should the player give them their swords, PorkChop and company will assist Jesse and Jack onto solid ground before leaving. Likewise, should the player refuse the deal, Jack will fall toward certain death, and Jesse is able to climb up safely. Nonetheless, the group (Jesse, Petra, Radar, Nurm/Lluna and/or Jack) make it to Fred's Keep.
At Fred's Keep, the group meet with Binta, who informs the group that they can't enter Fred's house unless they win a trivia contest, in which Jesse accepts. Jesse is able to talk with other people at Fred's Keep in order to obtain more information about Fred, such as his favorite tool, or his pet chicken's name. Eventually, if Jesse wins the contest against the defending champion, Kent, he is able to search the house freely for the weapon. Likewise, if Jesse loses the contest, he sneaks into the house to find the weapon. It turns out, however, that the "weapon" is actually Fred's personal diary. Nonetheless, Jesse convinces that the people of Fred's Keep will have a much better life above the bedrock. Binta makes preparations while the group (Jesse, Petra, Radar, Nurm/Lluna and/or Jack) head toward Romeoburg.
On the way to Romeoburg, the minecarts hit a few ditches, and Petra, Radar and Lluna/Nurm and/or Jack are hit with potions of slowness from the guard that Romeo (disguised as Jesse) summoned. After an altercation, it is revealed that the guard is Ivor, and Ivor reveals that Romeo gave him the Order's amulet in order to find "the fraud" (the real Jesse). Jesse sets up Ivor with a cover story of the player's choosing to fool Romeo, and in exchange, Ivor allows Jesse to use the amulet. Ivor then disappears in a puff of smoke, and the group continue toward Romeoburg.
At Romeoburg, the group are confronted by members of Romeo's supposed "army", which turns out to be just a record player. Having caught them in their act, the two (Soup and Cal) let them in and let them take on Romeo's Death Challenge, which involves a bunch of hostile mobs infused with lava. The group are able to survive the challenge, and make it toward a cabin. Inside the cabin, Petra talks to Jesse abut not being able to talk to each other, but the two reconcile and put aside their differences. The two then solve a puzzle to find Romeo's "weapon", which is known as "#Potato451", and make it back toward the Oasis.
Back at the Oasis, Jesse sets off the firework to signal the people at Fred's Keep, and gives Fred's "weapon" to Xara. Suddenly, a giant enderman appears and begins demolishing the portal, but it is quickly rebuilt by Jesse. The player can then choose to leave Radar behind as a distraction for the enderman or bring Radar with him, and the group goes through the portal. Back in the Overworld, the group (Jesse, Petra, Jack, Nurm/Lluna and Radar/Binta, Cam and Kent) show up at the Order's old temple. Jesse notices a firework going off, and sees that Beacontown is under Romeo's control.
In a post-credits scene, Lukas meets with Romeo (disguised as Jesse), and asks him where the others are.

title: Story Mode:Wither Storm
text:
This article is about one of the main antagonists in Minecraft: Story Mode. For the illness contracted from it, see Story Mode:Wither. For other uses, see Wither (disambiguation).
Details
Status: Destroyed
Aliases: Witherstorm The Storm A Living Storm ( PAMA )
Affiliations: Ivor (creator)
First appearance: The Order of the Stone
Latest appearance: Hero in Residence (flashback)
The wither storm or witherstorm was an enormous, advanced wither that served as the main antagonist for the first four episodes of Season One. It was created by Ivor to get revenge against the Order of the Stone. It was constructed with three wither skulls, three soul sand, and a command block in a cross shape.
Behavior
When initially spawned, the wither storm causes a shockwave which allows it to easily push people away. This variant of the wither follows the commands of the creator: Ivor.
It holds many attributes, unique only to a wither storm, being able to shoot flaming wither skulls and pull nearby blocks into itself.
However, this allows the wither storm to gain autonomy, growing stronger and escaping the influence of its creator.
The more blocks it absorbs, the more powerful it becomes, with it eventually gaining purple tractor beams to pull larger chunks of blocks. Through its newly gained power, a defence mechanism is used by covering the weak spot (the command block ) with its own body.
The path of the wither storm is not random, being programmed by Ivor to follow the amulet throughout the Overworld.
Biography
As Jesse and the others go into the EnderCon hall basement, they discover a configuration of soul sand and a mysterious block. After getting out of the basement, Ivor confronts Gabriel and unleashes a wither that he can control. However, the wither grew out of control and Ivor resorts to using a special potion to stop it. The potion did not work as it was revealed that Jesse or Axel has stolen the real potion, but the wither quickly protected its command block. After failing, Ivor ran out to escape and the group, together with Gabriel, follows.
The wither storm grew in size, destroying the town. It followed Jesse and the others at Gabriel's temple. Olivia and others managed to escape through a Nether portal, but Jesse, Lukas, Petra, and Gabriel stayed behind. Due to some mishaps, both Gabriel and Petra were both caught by the wither's tractor beams, and Jesse had to choose who to save, leaving the other to be saved by Lukas. Lukas froze in fear and Jesse was hit by a tentacle which threw them into Lukas and both were transported through the portal, leaving the unchosen person still stuck. As the group arrived in the temple of the Order of Stone, the wither storm mysteriously followed.
After recruiting Ellegaard or Magnus in their respective locations, the wither storm arrives, causing destruction in either Redstonia or Boom Town. The wither will also arrive at the temple of the Order if the group decided to stay and sleep.
After recruiting Soren, the group distracted the wither while Jesse prepared to use the formidi-bomb to destroy it. The wither storm seemingly died, and the people consumed by it emerged. As Jesse approached the corpse, he saw the command block still intact. The block activated and pulled Jesse, but they were saved by Gabriel or Petra. The block revived the wither storm.
The group escaped once again, planning to acquire an enchanted book that also contains the power of the command block itself. After getting an ultimate weapon, they all confronted the wither outside Soren's fortress.
They used the endermen to attack the wither, causing a hole to form which Jesse and Reuben go into.
Inside the wither storm, Jesse and Reuben found the command block. Jesse attacked it and the tentacles around it retaliated. The insides suddenly shifted, and the command block is now on the ceiling. After arriving at the top, Jesse accidentally dropped their weapon and Reuben retrieved it. However, as Reuben gave it to Jesse, he was caught by a wither storm head and fell into a hole. Jesse attacked the block once more and it was destroyed, destroying the wither in the process.
As the remains of the wither fell, Jesse found Reuben, who had been gravely injured. Reuben succumbed to his injuries, and people gathered around his remains.
Legacy
In remembrance of Reuben’s sacrifice, a memorial was erected in his honor. The event also led to the formation of the New Order of the Stone.
A town was established following the wither storm’s defeat, which later developed into Beacontown. The wither storm itself remained a significant historical event, recognized in Founding Day celebrations. Several posters at the event at Beacontown depict the wither storm.
Abilities
The wither storm is a very powerful creature. It gained its extraordinary capabilities with the help of the command block.
Suction
In its first form, the wither storm is able to absorb only objects while it doesn't have its tractor beam.
Flaming wither skull
Flaming wither skull
The wither storm is able to shoot wither skulls the same way the ordinary wither does. Each head can fire wither skulls through its mouth.
A flaming wither skull is an amplified, fiery version of the wither skull, with a unique texture and bigger explosion. It also leaves accumulated flames behind.
Growth
The wither storm has the ability to grow parts of its body, or grow additional tentacles and heads when consuming objects. In A Block and a Hard Place it is shown that the wither storm is able to grow additional heads and tentacles inside its own body so that it can protect the command block. This ability also heals the wither's wounds.
Tractor beam
Each individual eye of the wither storm can activate a tractor beam that pulls chunks and entities into itself unless the eye is damaged. These tractor beams also trigger the endermen 's agitated state due to them making direct eye contact with their face, and cause them to attack the wither storm.
Illness infliction
Main articles: Wither and Mad Wither
The wither storm transmits a severe form of wither, a long-term version that can only be cured by destroying the command block. The wither storm also causes mad wither, which leads to retrograde amnesia in the affected individual.
Tentacles
The wither storm has several long tentacles that it uses to attack, capture prey, and destroy blocks around to help it get to its destinations. The tight grip of these tentacles can destroy even obsidian.
Revivability
The wither storm can be revived if its command block is left intact. Despite a point-blank blast from the formidi-bomb leaving the wither storm seemingly "dead", the command block reactivates which revives the wither.
Splitting
Thanks to the command block, segments severed from the wither storm can breathe new life as two progenies linked to the command block housed inside the main body. Once the command block is destroyed, its progenies die with the original.
Amulet tracking
The wither storm is able to track the Order's amulet as long as it is not in the Nether or the End.

Trivia
Inside the wither storm can be found decayed citizens, some of them being named characters. They are Jens, Lydia, Otis, Owen, Schoolboy, and the human Reuben.
The terms "wither storm" and "witherstorm" are interchangeable; in fact, both terms were used to refer to it in the game. The former was used in the first and second episodes, and the latter was used in third to fifth episodes.
Several years after the game, the graphic novel Minecraft: Volume 1 featured its own take on the wither, which is similarly associated with tentacles.

Aiden is a character in Minecraft: Story Mode, who caused Reuben to run away from the EnderCon Building Competition in The Order of the Stone, and the main antagonist in Order Up!. He was one of the four members of the Ocelots, a team of builders rival to Jesse's gang. He later forms his own group, the Blaze Rods. He is voiced by Matthew Mercer.
Appearance
His skin is a desaturated white color in the game version, but seems more saturated and darkened in the Netflix version. He has olive green eyes and short brown hair. He wears a black leather jacket with the face of an ocelot on it. Under this, he wears a green Magnus themed shirt with yellow buttons similar to Magnus' armor. Later in Order Up!, he wears armor similar to that of a Roman gladiator, which also resembles the arms and legs of Soren's armor.His jacket is now adorned with a logo featuring two blaze rods instead of an ocelot face.
Personality
He is very self-centered and egotistical, being rude to Jesse and their group, and risking Reuben's life in the first episode. In A Block and a Hard Place, if Jesse lets Lukas leave on good terms and lets him go after the rest of the Ocelots, Aiden will help Jesse's gang destroy the wither storm by distracting it with fireworks. Throughout Order Up!, he is shown as manipulative, selfish, bitter, and merciless until the very end. Near the end of Order Up!, if Jesse threw Aiden over the edge of Sky City, Aiden will hold a grudge until the end. However when Jesse decided to take him to safety when he surrendered, he is shown to be able to repent and then says he will become a writer and start over, he even sent Jesse a letter in Above and Beyond that stated he wants to redeem himself. The letter was sent regardless of the final choices in Order Up!
Biography

If you stay in Beacontown in Season Two, Radar states that "You received a letter from someone named Aiden? Something about wanting to redeem himself."

title: Story Mode:The Last Place You Look
text:
Verify the plot details if they are interpreted correctly from the game and separate the plot by chapters based on the achievements.
Details
Episode: 3
Season: 1
Release date: November 24, 2015
Written by: Michael Choung, Laura Jacqmin, Eric Stirpe, and Tim Williams
Directed by: Jonathan Stauder
The Last Place You Look, also known as Episode 3, is the third episode of Minecraft: Story Mode. It was released on November 24, 2015. The episode follows Jesse and their friends as they search for Soren, the final missing member of the Order of the Stone, in hopes of finding a way to defeat the wither storm.
Synopsis
“ Jesse and friends follow the trail of Soren, the final member of the Order, to The End, and enlist his help in taking down the Witherstorm once and for all. „ — In-game description.
Plot
After the potion effect wears off, Jesse and their friends have to think of another way to get to Soren. Thinking quickly, Jesse uses the amulet and finds out that Soren is deep underground. They dig a hole to a passage which leads them to a mob grinder. A creeper blows up a portion of the passage, causing the amulet to slip out of Jesse's hands and fall onto a platform. After fighting some hostile mobs, Jesse/Lukas successfully retrieves the amulet, but slips on some water, causing them to fall toward death. Luckily, a creeper blows up the "crusher" just in time so Jesse and their friends fall down safely. From there, they are sucked in by gusts of water to the latter portion of the grinder, causing them to fight even more hostile mobs. Barely escaping, the group find an end portal and jump in.
In the End, the group is confronted by several endermen, but they manage to find Soren heading up a staircase. The group decides that the only way to get to him is to safely walk past the endermen, which they do. At the top of the staircase is an area made of wool, but Lukas goes off in a rage, thinking this area was all there was. Lukas storms off as Jesse finds a secret door, which leads them to Soren's house.
Inside his house, Jesse and Olivia stumble upon some music playing and a voice behind a door. Opening it reveals a jukebox playing a recording. Frustrated, Olivia searches around the room to look for some clues while Jesse listens to the rest of the records. One of the records talks about Soren making a suit to disguise himself as an enderman, which Jesse later finds on their own. In a closet, Jesse also finds some blueprints, which Olivia believes are being used to teach the endermen to build. After collecting the five clay bricks inside the room, Jesse goes outside to take the sixth brick from an enderman, causing the enderman to become "frustrated". Acting quickly, Jesse completes the crafting recipe, only to be knocked out by another person in an enderman suit. Back inside, it is revealed that the person is indeed Soren. Soren feels suspicious at first, but immediately recognizes the Order when Jesse mentions the Amulet. Gabriel (or Petra) and Soren happily reunite, but their celebration is cut short as endermen teleport into his house, causing them to escape the End.
Back in the Overworld, the group use a set of minecarts to head back up to the surface. Upon arriving, Magnus and Ellegaard are there to assist the group. As the wither storm approaches, both Ellegaard and Magnus request Jesse to take their set of armor. Upon choosing either, Jesse makes haste to craft the formidi-bomb. Upon doing so, they throw it at the wither storm just before it explodes. In the aftermath, the wither storm is presumed dead, but either Ellegaard or Magnus also die due to a blow from one of the Storm's tentacles. Jesse then helps out some survivors, but finds out that the command block wasn't destroyed, allowing the wither storm to come back to life. Surprisingly, either Gabriel or Petra (whichever the player abandoned at the temple) shows up to assist them. It is also revealed that they have amnesia, and have forgotten their names.

Radar is a major supporting character. He is Jesse's assistant and intern in Minecraft: Story Mode - Season Two.
Appearance

Radar has dark-tan skin, black eyes, and black hair. He typically wears black glasses, blue jeans, a green hoodie with white drawstrings, a light brown vest, and a blue tie.
He also has a black tattoo of the Ender Dragon in a Chinese ink style on his left arm, only visible in his Prison and Armored outfits.
Personality
Radar is obsessed with Jesse and the New Order of the Stone. He enjoys working for Jesse and likes to help them. If Jesse lets him temporarily run Beacontown or watch over the Giant Clock, he gets excited. Radar is also nervous around the New Order of the Stone, evidenced by how he acts nervously around them at the beginning of Hero in Residence.
He is also very organized, seen when he determinately says " There's no schedule; everyone's running around unorganized " which he doesn't like as shown when he complains how disorganized the Sunshine Institute is, and when he deems the armors in Romeoburg barbaric because of how unorganized they are.
At first, Radar dislikes adventuring, shown in Hero in Residence when he tells Jesse how dangerous adventuring is. However, during the group's journey to the Icy Palace of Despair, Radar slowly becomes stronger. After seeing the natures of the Sunshine Institute, he becomes "Prison Radar" and starts to play tough, although his organized personality and admiration for Jesse are still often shown at times. Eventually, in Above and Beyond, he returns to be the organized fan of Jesse. He determinately acts more aggressive in A bove and Beyond.

Trivia
The game makes a reference to Ben 10 when Jesse tells Radar "It's hero time!" when escaping the golems, which is Ben 10's catchphrase. This is because both are voiced by Yuri Lowenthal.
Yuri Lowenthal also voices Willy.
Eric Stirpe revealed the following about Radar on Tumblr:
Radar has been working for Jesse for about a year by the time of Hero in Residence.
Radar is both Jesse's assistant and intern.
Radar's favorite animals are rabbits.
Radar is East Asian.
Radar's tattoo is a Chinese ink-style drawing of the Ender Dragon.
If Jesse looks at the octopus statue in Hero in Residence, Radar will state that he loves seafood.
It is completely unknown how Radar could enter the Terminal Zone (if Jesse left him behind in Below the Bedrock ), as the portal entrance was concealed by Romeo's bedrock layer.
Radar is supposed to sing a song called "Taste My Bravery" in an episode, but it wasn't able to make it in.

title: Dungeons:Arch-Illager
text:
Note: Remove the background noise of the exclusive sounds. See § Opening Cinematic
Health points: 3200
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Enemies
Mob category: Illager, Boss
Attack strength: Projectile: 45 Melee Attack: 75
Spawn: Obsidian Pinnacle
The Arch-Illager, also known by his birth name Archie, is one of the two main antagonists (alongside the Orb of Dominance ) of the game. He is the founder and leader of the Illager Empire in the game, and is a proxy for the Orb of Dominance to carry out its goals.
Spawning
Obsidian Pinnacle
Behavior
The Arch-Illager has seven behaviors. If a hero or summon comes too close to him, he begins repeatedly teleporting around the arena and firing blasts after each teleport. His health pool is the second lowest of any boss in the game, although this is mitigated by the deadly Heart of Ender battle that takes place after his health is depleted. Additionally, the Arch-Illager, when he isn't a boss, flies around summoning bosses and battles to try and thwart the hero's efforts to stop him.
Basic attack
The Arch-Illager fires three purple energy blasts in a horizontal swath within 2500 units (approximately 25 blocks) of a hero or summon. Deals a small amount of damage if it hits (45 per energy blast in code). Used extremely often.
Each energy blast has an angle span of 20 degrees, a 0.5-second delay, and will automatically disappear after 20 seconds.
Melee attack
The Arch-Illager uses a melee attack to pushback heroes within 220 units (approximately 2.2 blocks), dealing a small amount of damage (75 in code) and knockback heroes.
As the Arch-Illager teleports away when the hero gets close, and this behavior also uses the "Basic attack" animation, this behavior is very rare and difficult to trigger.
Teleport
When a hero comes within 300 units (approximately 3 blocks) of the Arch-Illager, the Arch-Illager teleports 1350 units (approximately 13.5 blocks) in a random area around the hero to avoid taking much damage. Similarly to the enderman, the arch-illager can also hide in teleportation and not reappear instantly.Teleport In and Out both have a duration of 0.9 seconds.
Enhance
The Arch-Illager can enchant up to 5 hostile mobs within 1100 units (approximately 11 blocks) of himself, similar to the enchanter. The enchantment has 0.9 seconds delay and 2 seconds attack rate. Enchanted mobs will only take 0.333x damage and can deal 1.75x damage, and their speed will also be increased to 2x.
However, if the Arch-Illager teleports, the enchanting is broken off the mob.
Summon circle
Since the Arch-Illager is not much of a warrior, he is capable of summoning a group of Illagers to assist him, usually a circle of ten to twenty vindicators around the hero.
This skill has a 28-second cooldown.
Summon line
The Arch-Illager can also summon a line of twenty pillagers through the center of the arena.
This skill has a 28-second cooldown.
Summon single
The Arch-Illager rarely summons a single redstone golem. This mob is not powerful unlike other redstone golems, and unlike the Arch-Illager's other summoned mobs, it does not die once he is defeated. While this attack is used sparingly, it is his most powerful attack.
This skill has a 28-second cooldown.
Defeated in a whirlwind
Once the Arch-Illager has taken enough damage, his staff slips out of his hands, sucks him in, and absorbs him while transforming into the Heart of Ender.
Appearance
In the opening cinematic, Archie has ragged and torn clothes and is holding a torch. When he is crowned as the Arch-Illager, he has blue robes that are similar to the evocation robe with a large chain with a ruby on it and a crown with a sapphire and a ruby on it. He also carries a staff to hold the Orb of Dominance. The Arch-Illager is also the only illager to have a horizontal head and a smaller height.
In the arena, the Arch-Illager will appear in the sky above the arena in its "ghost" form and then summon enemies. In "ghost" form, its clothes will glow, and its body will have a purple enchantment glow and special effects.
In the arcade edition, the top of the Arch-Illager's crown lacks a texture, only appearing black.
Personality
As being an illager. Archie seems to have been quite cowardly before he was corrupted by the Orb of Dominance. He often would not run into the face of danger. However, in certain situations, he has the potential to be a strong warrior. He also is shown to have violent tendencies in certain situations, such as when Thord causes him to get exiled, and when Salah causes Smacker to get him thrown out of the village. At one point, he briefly considers executing Walda for her part in Thord’s betrayal.
Biography

Instructions: Explain more of Rise, including his partnership with the Nameless One
Early life
A Woodland Mansion
Archie was born within a woodland mansion under the leadership of Walda, an evoker. His parents died in a patrol when he was young, leaving him orphaned at a young age. Forced to grow up alone, Archie had a rough childhood, hardly having any help from his illager tribe and being the target of harassment from Thord, another evoker his age. Sometime early in his life, he developed ideas that if the illager tribes of the Overworld banded together, they could defeat the heroes that constantly hunted them. Due to being an outsider, the other illagers disregarded his radical beliefs as they went against the common ideology that illagers are power hungry and combative by nature, and that they only follow the strongest illager as their leader. The other illagers believed that if they ever tried to unite, it would just be coup after coup.
Life in exile
Karl ("Smacker") is one of the five heroes in story
Archie’s life takes a turn when he was forcibly brought into the patrol led by Thord, responsible for clearing the nearby monsters. Unexpectedly, a hero named Karl arrived and turned the tide, slaughtering both the undead and illagers. Only Archie and Thord surviving, he blamed the incident on Archie for not helping, even though he was told to stay out of the fight. After they returned to the woodland mansion, Thord reported to Walda of his view of the incident. Thord’s lies push Archie to react by pushing Thord in front of Walda, who fell on purpose and pretended to be in pain. In response, Archie became exiled from the tribe.
Archie searches for a new home
After struggling to survive in the wilderness, Archie discovered a village on the squid coast. There the inhabitants proved distrustful of him, save for a villager named Yumi who comes to respect and vouch for him. Thanks to her, he lived in the village for anywhere from a month to a year, until Karl happened upon the town. The village spokesperson, Salah, got the hero’s attention drawn to Archie and quickly threatened to kill him, since he could never feel safe in a village if an illager was there. Yumi fought to let him stay, but she had no choice but to tell Archie to run.
Back on the run, by sheer chance Archie happened upon a mountain, inside discovering the tomb of the Orb of Dominance. Freeing it from the depths, it promised to give Archie anything he could ever want. Repeatedly telling him it was Archie's destiny to rule the world, Archie would unite many illager tribes under his rule, taking the title in the process.
Archie finds the Orb of Dominance
Illager empire
The Orb constructed Highblock Keep as Archie’s home, and gave him six redstone golems to protect him. However, these golems could not last forever, so he'd need a forge to make more, and a mine to gather redstone. The illagers were willing to work for him; at least once the Orb proved that it would not allow anyone to take it from Archie. The Orb insisted they would need to attack the villages of the continent so the militant illagers would "not get bored." Walda crowns Archie as the Arch-Illager
The Arch-Illager takes his revenge
"Smacker" was scared away by Archie's custom-made redstone monstrosity
It was a downward spiral, as one thing required another, each more evil than the last. He eventually killed Thord, who attempted to betray him once again. By Archie’s coronation, the illagers had no qualms at his leadership, bowing at him out of respect. Now mad with power, he captures Salah as a prisoner, and tried to convince Yumi to join, who refused. Pleading, Yumi almost broke through the veil, but the Orb, now partially controlling Archie's thoughts, would not let her. With the new title of the Arch-Illager, Archie vowed to make all of the Overworld suffer for taking away the peaceful life he could have had.
Minecraft Dungeons
During his reign, he would collaborate with the witches of the Soggy Swamp to obtain large potion supplies, as well as order the development of the redstone mines to use his villager slaves to mine redstone, and construct the fiery forge to refine the gathered redstone into the Redstone Monstrosity to enforce his rule. Eventually, the Arch-Illager would attempt to betray the Nameless One, launching a raid on the desert temple to try and take his staff to gain the ability to summon the undead army for himself. Each of these plans, however, would be foiled by the heroes.
Eventually, once the heroes reach the Arch-Illager's throne room in Highblock Halls, he summons one last attack as the heroes reach him, before retreating to the Obsidian Pinnacle. After the heroes finally reach him on the highest point of the castle, the Arch-Illager fights the hero(s) himself. After having been heavily wounded, Archie is sucked toward the Orb of Dominance, which proceeds to manifest itself as the Heart of Ender. Once enough damage has been done to the embodiment, the heart of ender dissipates, rendering the Orb of Dominance extremely unstable.
The heroes find the Arch-Illager struggling to contain the Orb of Dominance a few moments before it shatters, knocking Archie down and relieving him of its control. Without the Orb of Dominance to give him power, Archie becomes afraid of the approaching heroes. However, they unexpectedly offer kindness and help him up. Archie then celebrates the destruction of the Orb of Dominance, and by extension the persona of the Arch-Illager, with the heroes.
After the Vengeful Heart of Ender and the Orb of Dominance was destroyed, it's currently unknown what happened to the Arch-Illager afterwards.

title: Dungeons:Orb of Dominance
text:
This article is about the orb in Minecraft Dungeons. For other namesakes, see Orb of Dominance.

Note:
Add the enderman sounds that the Orb of Dominance produces in the opening cinematic.
The lore sections needs to cite from individual chapters of the prequel novel.
Status: Shattered
Designation: Sentient
Owner: The Seer ​ [ formerly ] Arch-Illager ​ [ formerly ]
Destroyed: Echoing Void
First appearance: Minecraft Dungeons: The Rise of the Arch-Illager
Latest appearance: Minecraft Legends
The Orb of Dominance is a sentient object in Minecraft Dungeons that manipulated the Arch-Illager into believing that it could help him reshape the world into a society in which he would be accepted. It is the disguised form of the Heart of Ender and is the overarching antagonist of the game.
After the heroes defeat the Arch Illager, the Orb of Dominance shatters, but the Orb's shards scatter around different islands, spreading corruption where they land. The Minecraft Dungeons DLC primarily focuses on the heroes' quest to destroy these remnants.
