Personality
Throughout the game and the prequel novel, the Orb of Dominance appears to be intensely manipulative. This was made apparent in its corruption of Archie in how it warped Archie's desire of being accepted within a society. The orb's corruption of Archie also showed that the orb was intensely methodical in that the orb was specific in its many suggestions to Archie and it was not in any way at all concerned with the possibility of failing in corrupting Archie. The orb initially showed Archie the kindness that he so desperately desired, such as when it summoned a plate of food when he was hungry and being willing to listen and support Archie when he talks about his personal issues. The orb's kinder side is also seen when it suggested to Archie that he should use the six redstone golems to clear a path for him rather than doing so on his own. Upon gaining Archie's trust, the orb then instilled into Archie a need for vengeance against the individuals who had ostracized him from society. It then convinced Archie that in order for him to get his revenge, they needed to build an army. The orb's corruption gets to the point where the more Archie confides in and discusses with the orb, the more control the orb gains over its suggestions to Archie. This is seen in how it eventually causes Archie to become increasingly paranoid of betrayal. It uses Archie's dislike and fear of Thord, and his distrust and suspicions of Walda's attempted manipulations of him to instill in Archie a paranoia of betrayal. This is explicitly shown in how by the end of the novel, the orb had effectively eliminated every individual that could potentially stop its corruption of Archie, such as getting Archie to attack the village a second time to take prisoners, resulting in Archie's only friend, Yumi, blaming the attack on him since she had no knowledge of the orb's corruption. This paranoia of betrayal is also implied in the desert temple mission in the game itself with how the story description states that Archie was planning a raid on the desert temple to take the Nameless One's staff, despite previously making an uneasy alliance with the Nameless One in the prequel novel. It is implied that the paranoia of betrayal that the orb had instilled within Archie led him believing that the only way to prevent the Nameless One from betraying him was to betray their uneasy alliance first. Just like how it took a few non-genuine acts of kindness from the orb to ensnare Archie, all it took to free him from the orb was being spared by the heroes atop the Obsidian Pinnacle, allowing him to join their society.
Powers and abilities
The Orb of Dominance is used by the Arch-Illager to summon ambushes, bosses, and minions. He also uses it to enhance mobs and fire purple blasts at heroes. The Orb itself was capable of understanding Illageralt and the language of the hero Karl. The Orb withheld from Archie that it could act as a translater until his attack on the Squid Coast with the Redstone Monstrosity.
Biography
Spoiler warning! This section contains detailed information about Minecraft Dungeons and Minecraft Dungeons: The Rise of the Arch-Illager that may spoil your enjoyment of it. Read at your own risk!
Minecraft Dungeons: The Rise of the Arch-Illager
In legends dating from long before the events of the Arch-Illager Saga, the Orb of Dominance was allegedly first seen in the Nether in the possession of The Seer, who used it to corrupt a beacon.
Sometime after the piglin invasion, the Orb of Dominance somehow ended up within a sanctuary hidden in the mountains, under the watchful gaze of six redstone golems. For a long time, it would fester there until its existence was all but forgotten.
Many years later, an outcast illager named Archie, banished from his tribe, would come across the now crumbling sanctuary, seeking shelter. Sensing Archie's troubled thoughts and despair, the orb called out to him, entrancing him with promises to solve his problems. At first, Archie tried to resist its beckoning, but soon gave in, releasing the malevolent orb from its pedestal, throwing Archie into a state of unconsciousness. Archie woke up to the orb telling him that they had to leave the sanctuary if he wanted to fix his problems. Before they left, Archie became distracted by the sight of the sanctuary's redstone golems, which had fallen into disrepair like the ruins they guarded. Using its dark power, the orb reactivated the golems, giving them a new purpose, presenting them as a gift to Archie. The orb then ripped out chunks of gold and iron ore, forging a staff for Archie, who rested the orb upon the top of the staff. The orb then summoned food and bedding for Archie after sensing his weakness. The next morning, Archie and the orb were unexpectedly attacked by some husks that were drawn to their recent activity in the sanctuary ruins. Just before Archie prepared to use the staff as a melee weapon, the orb fired energy which obliterated the husks. Afterward, the orb and the redstone golems cleared a path down the mountain for Archie. Upon sensing Archie's desire for vengeance against his tormentors, the orb informed him that they need an army.
After leaving the mountains, Archie, the orb, and the golems made their way to the eastern coast of the Mainland. There, the orb created a new home for Archie, calling it Highblock Keep. The orb then took Archie to the ramparts of the castle, which was known as the Obsidian Pinnacle. Upon the pinnacle, the orb granted Archie a vision of the evoker, Thord, leading several illagers in a desperate struggle against an undead horde lead by a necromancer in the Cacti Canyon.
As Archie watched Thord and the other illagers, the orb suggested that the illagers could serve as their army. Sensing Archie's hesitation, the orb advised that he could send the redstone golems to assist the illagers and Archie agreed to go himself. As they made their way through the cacti canyon, Archie questioned whether the illagers would attack him on sight, though the orb reassures him that all who would oppose him would be destroyed. As they continued searching, the orb told him that they need a large source of heat to forge new redstone golems.
Archie decided to rest, yet before he could, the orb alerted him that the illagers were nearby. At the orb's insistence, Archie defeated the undead horde attacking the illagers.
With the illager tribe heading to Highblock Keep, Archie used the orb to furnish Highblock Keep, before heading to a nearby volcano where the orb managed to create a new redstone golem from the local minerals.
Leaving the new redstone golem to mine out the volcano underground cavern, Archie began to leave, much to the orb's protests.
With the illagers at Highblock Keep, Archie tried to provide comfort, but the orb informed him that it would make him look weak, giving Thord the potential possibility of trying to usurp him.
The next day, Archie returned to the volcano to begin construction on the fiery forge. He used the orb to create a new redstone golem in front of the illagers, who applauded his act of creation. Archie then returned back to Highblock Castle in time for the orb to create a throne room for Archie. Thord then remarked that despite the orb, Archie was still weak. The orb impulsively advised Archie to kill the evoker for his lack of loyalty, but Archie chose to ignore the advice.
During Archie's attack on the Squid Coast, the illagers were ambushed by the undead, resulting in two skeletons abducting Archie. The undead had Archie brought to the Desert Temple, where he obtained an audience with the Nameless One, the reanimated king of the Overworld, who reigned thousands of years ago during the orb's original rampage. The Nameless One revealed that he wanted the orb so that the undead would no longer burn in sunlight. Archie agreed to grant them immunity, in exchange for an alliance between the undead and illagers.
Upon returning to Highblock Castle, Archie was greeted by his former matriarch, Walda, who informed him that Thord was overseeing security at the fiery forge. Archie departed to the obsidian pinnacle where the orb showed Archie that Thord had commandeered a redstone golem to steal a shipment of ore.
Archie soon let Highblock Castle and found Thord struggling to move the shipment of ore down the lava lake. Upon spotting Archie, Thord tried attacking the illager outcast, but the orb protected Archie. Ultimately loyal to the Orb of Dominance, Thord's commandeered redstone golem turned on the evoker. Thord tried to beg Archie for mercy but the illager outcast did not grant him any for his treachery. It is unknown whether Thord was killed directly by the golems or fell into the lava lake.
To celebrate the death of his tormentor, Archie used the orb to create something magnificent: the redstone monstrosity. Archie returned to Highblock Castle to question Walda, correctly predicting that she orchestrated the failed heist. Walda changed the subject by proposing that a coronation ceremony be held for Archie.
The ceremony went without trouble with Archie declaring himself as the "Arch-Illager". Afterward, the Arch-Illager retreated to obsidian pinnacle where the orb showed him a vision of a reinforced squid coast and the homes of his enemies. The Arch-Illager commanded Walda to burn the houses to the ground. Walda, who now knew that the Arch-Illager could not be usurped, loyally complied.
Deep down, Archie questioned if he was doing the right thing but the orb said that they could not tolerate non-loyalty from any living being as they traveled west.
Now at the outskirts of the squid coast, the Arch-Illager smiled at the sight of smoke rising from the heroes' houses burning. Once again, Archie questioned his actions deep down but the orb reminded him of his past and told him that this was his retribution.
As the Arch-Illager basked in the flames of the squid coast burning down, what little was left of Archie was snuffed out by the orb. Finally, after thousands of years, the Orb of Dominance shall rule the Overworld.
Minecraft Dungeons
Minecraft Dungeons focuses on the heroes' quest to free the Overworld from the grasp of the Arch-Illager, and by extension, the Orb of Dominance.
Throughout the main storyline, the Arch-Illager carries the Orb on a scepter. With the Orb's power, he can appear before the heroes in various places and summon enemies.
The heroes start by freeing some villagers from Creeper Woods and warning some others in Pumpkin Pastures. They defeat the Illagers in the Redstone Mines and overload the cores of the Fiery Forge, hampering construction of the Redstone Golems. The Arch-Illager also attempts to obtain the Soggy Swamp brews and the Nameless One 's staff (breaking his earlier tenuous alliance with the Nameless One), but the heroes manage to get to both of them first and destroy them.
Finally, while the Illagers celebrate their conquests at Highblock Halls, the heroes launch an all-out attack. They find the Arch-Illager, who summons many guards to his aid, but the heroes defeat them all. The heroes pursue the Arch-Illager to the end of the Obsidian Pinnacle, and they engage in battle. During the fight, the Arch-Illager uses the Orb to teleport, shoot projectiles, and summon minions.
When the Arch-Illager is defeated, the Orb reveals its true form - the Heart of Ender - and attacks the heroes itself. Despite this, the heroes manage to overpower the Heart of Ender and defeat it. It briefly returns to its Orb form before exploding. After the heroes leave, though, the shards begin to vibrate, showing that the Orb had not been completely destroyed.
Five of the Orb's shards are spread across the Overworld and began to spread corruption where they land. Four of the shards land on the island realms:
One shard lands in the Overgrown Temple and corrupts the Jungle Abomination. It also ends up creating the Treetop Tangle.
One shard lands in the Lone Fortress and corrupts the Wretched Wraith.
One shard lands in the Gale Sanctum and corrupts the Tempest Golem.
One shard lands in the Abyssal Monument and corrupts the Ancient Guardian.
The heroes manage to track down and destroy each of these shards, as well as the beings that they corrupted.
The fifth and final shard ends up buried somewhere, but it summons two Endermen to retrieve it and take it to the Stronghold. From there, the Endermen use the active End Portal to bring it to the End (presumably where it was born). At the same time, it sends six Endersent to take the Eyes of Ender from the portal; deactivating it. The six Endersent then scatter across the Overworld.
The heroes defeat the Endersent and retrieve the Eyes of Ender, using them to activate the End Portal once again. By this time, though, the shard has regenerated into the imperfect form of the Vengeful Heart of Ender, allowing it to crawl around the islands. The heroes track it through the End Wilds and to the Broken Citadel, where it enters a void pool and finally becomes the complete Vengeful Heart of Ender. The final battle ensues, but the heroes manage to prevail again, and the final remnant of the Orb of Dominance is destroyed, freeing the Overworld from its grasp once and for all.
Confirmed dominations
Ancient Guardian
Archie

Jungle Abomination
Forms
Heart of Ender — The first form of the orb, revealing itself on the Obsidian Pinnacle as a last resort for preventing the defeat of the Arch-Illager and fall of the Illager empire.
Imperfect Heart of Ender — The second form of the orb, which the final shard regenerated into after it was returned to the End.
Vengeful Heart of Ender — The third and final form of the orb, formed after the imperfect form climbed into the void pool in the Broken Citadel.
Sounds

Archie discovers an object which whispers to him promises of power: the Orb of Dominance. With it, Archie realizes he can wield incredible magic and reshape a world that turned its back on him. All he needs to do is exactly what it tells him. After all, it's called the Orb of Dominance for a reason, right? But is it named for the way Archie uses it—or is it using him? „ — Minecraft Dungeons: The Rise of the Arch-Illager synopsis

title: Dungeons:Mooshroom Monstrosity
text:
Not to be confused with MCD:Redstone Monstrosity.

Health points: 25000
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Golem, Boss
Attack strength: Melee Attack: 200 Aoe Attack: 60
The Mooshroom Monstrosity is a secret boss and a variant of the Redstone Monstrosity in Minecraft Dungeons. It can be found in the??? secret mission and in The Tower.
Spawning
The missions, secret missions, sub dungeons, and/or sub-missions where the Mooshroom Monstrosity can spawn.
???
The Tower
Behavior
The Mooshroom Monstrosity has three behaviors.
Slam attack
The mooshroom monstrosity smashes both of its fists on the ground, creating a large four block shockwave in a radius that does heavy damage to and knocks back any other entities in its range. This attack is the one used most often when a hero gets in range.
Mooshroom summon
The mooshroom monstrosity pounds the ground with both fists as its body glows. Twelve fissures appear around the arena, each spawning a mooshroom. The mooshroom monstrosity has a twenty second cooldown after using this pattern. Each mooshroom has 1/80 of the boss's health pool.
Strong attack
The mooshroom monstrosity's most powerful attack is to shoot out a line of seven flaming mushroom projectiles from both its hands. When the mushroom hits an object, it explodes in a small radius which deals 90% damage per flaming mushroom. It is more commonly used when the hero is in a certain range from the boss.
Health
The Mooshroom Monstrosity has 25,000 HP, which is the highest health points among all bosses. On Default I, all mobs have 9.6% health and 12% damage of what is set in the code.

title: Dungeons:Nameless One
text:
Not to be confused with MCD:Necromancer.
For other uses, see Skeleton (disambiguation).
Health points: 6500
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Undead, Boss
Attack strength: 250
THE NAMELESS ONE The Nameless One is a boss variant of a necromancer. It summons its skeleton vanguard to attack heroes before joining the fray and targeting you with large, slow-moving projectiles. It can also confuse heroes by creating mirror images of itself.
The Nameless One is the necromancer boss variant found at the end of the Desert Temple mission of Minecraft Dungeons. It once ruled a long-forgotten nameless kingdom, and wields an enchanted staff used to perform necromancy.
Spawning
Desert Temple ( Boss )
Tower ( Boss )
Biography
Spoiler warning! This section contains detailed information about Minecraft Dungeons that may spoil your enjoyment of it. Read at your own risk!
In The Rise of the Arch-Illager, when Archie is leading the Illagers to the Squid Coast, he decides to take a wide arc around the Desert Temple. Unknown to Archie, despite this, the Nameless One knows of the abilities of the Orb of Dominance and sends husks and skeletons to follow the Illagers under the cover of darkness. After the Illagers launch their raid on Squid Coast and Archie is confronted by Yumi, the Undead army appears. They launch their own raid, attacking Illagers, villagers, and heroes alike. Archie is shot twice by two skeletons and falls unconscious. Archie wakes up in the midst of being carried to the desert temple and the leading necromancer knocks him out with its staff. He wakes in a prison cell held by a different necromancer who leads him captive to the throne room. In the throne room, Archie meets the Nameless One in person. The Nameless One makes it clear that it desires the Orb of Dominance to make the Undead army immune to sunlight. Archie makes it clear that he will not part with the Orb of Dominance. With both of them trying to gain the upper hand in the tense conversation, they eventually come to an agreement: Archie can use the Orb of Dominance to make the Undead immune to the sunlight and the Nameless One will allow Archie to command the Undead army.
When the heroes first arrive at the Desert Temple, the in-game description of the mission implies that the Nameless One and Archie turned on each other. However, it is vague on which one of the two was the first to betray the other. The description seems to lean more on Archie being the betrayer and desiring the Nameless One’s staff. In the Desert Temple, the heroes see the Nameless One asleep on its throne, surrounded by several sleeping vanguards. When the heroes attempt to grab its staff, it awakens and attempts to fend off the heroes. Once the Nameless One is defeated, the heroes are free to destroy the staff and leave the Desert Temple.
Behavior
The Nameless One has five behaviors.
Awake source
When the hero first initiates the boss fight, the Nameless One slowly rises from its tomb-like throne, opening its eyes.
Basic attack
The Nameless One fires large, slow-moving green projectiles towards its target current location. Deals heavy damage if it hits. This is its most common attack.
Summon
After a charging animation, the Nameless One summons a wall of seven vanguards between itself and the hero. Used more sparsely.
Strong attack
The Nameless One summons one to seven false kings, which fire one green beam before disappearing. If there are two or more false kings, they will teleport to surround the hero and all fire green beams at the hero.
Teleport
When not attacking, it teleports away from the hero and hovers in one spot. But when the hero attacks it first, then it attacks.
False king
A false king is a mirror image of the Nameless One that is summoned to confuse the hero. They often teleport in a ring formation around the hero and fire one green beam at them, before disappearing. It is not possible to attack these false kings.
Health
The Nameless One has 6,500 HP in the code, the first health of any boss than the unbreakable ones. On Default I, all mobs have 9.6% health and 12% damage of what is set in the code.

title: Dungeons:Tempest Golem
text:
This page describes content that is a part of the Howling Peaks DLC.
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Golem, Boss
The Tempest Golem is a golem and the boss of the Howling Peaks DLC found in the Gale Sanctum mission of Minecraft Dungeons.
Story
Spoiler warning! This section contains detailed information about Minecraft Dungeons that may spoil your enjoyment of it. Read at your own risk!
Upon the shattering of the Orb of Dominance, one of its shards landed atop the Howling Peaks. From there, it corrupted the ancient Tempest Golem within the Gale Sanctum. From this sanctum, it brought harsh storms and dark clouds across the summit, threatening to blow the entire world to rubble. Amidst the snow and wind, illagers and heroes began climbing the mountain, racing to its peak. These heroes repelled the illagers, stormed into the tempest chamber, and destroyed the tempest golem, bringing a soothing breeze across the mountain.
Spawning
Gale Sanctum
Higher Gauntlet ( Boss )
The Tower
Top Floor ( Boss )
Tempest Golem Boss Floor ( Boss )
Behavior
The Tempest Golem never moves from where it spawned, always remaining immobile similar to the Corrupted Cauldron. It has 4 different attacks. Chilling, Satchel of the Elements, and other freezing abilities do not affect the Tempest Golem's attack speed (However, it does work on the other DLC bosses). The Tempest Golem is invulnerable to damage unless the two pillars nearby are deactivated, causing it to be stunned temporarily.
The Tempest Golem's melee, shield, and lightning attacks can knock away other mobs, but not damage them.
Sword attack
If a hero goes to either side of the Tempest Golem, it smashes the ground with one of its sword arms, creating a small shockwave around it and deal some or a bit of damage to the hero.
Shield attack
If a hero goes in front of the Tempest Golem's body, it quickly opens its two shield panels, dealing little damage and knocking them away. It then closes them shortly after.
Electric tornado
Occasionally, the Tempest Golem summons a lightning bolt to attack a hero. This is signified by a blue square on the ground which follows them for 3 seconds. After 3 seconds, lightning strikes down on that square, dealing heavy damage.
Squall golem activation
After the Tempest Golem is stunned and gets back up, or when it is initially activated, 2 squall golems from the back of the arena activate and attack the hero. There are 8 squall golems total in the back.
Health
The Tempest Golem has 18,000 HP. On Default I, all mobs have 9.6% health and 12% damage of what is set in the code.

title: Dungeons:Redstone Monstrosity
text:
Not to be confused with MCD:Redstone Golem,  MCD:Mooshroom Monstrosity,  MCD:Obsidian Monstrosity, or  MCD:Redstone Ministrosity.
Health points: 12000 Immune lava damage Redstone Core deals 15 times the damage to it
Behavior: Hostile
Classification: Golem Boss
Attack strength: Strong attack anticipation:250 Strong attack (Summon):20 Serialized explosion damage:125
The redstone monstrosity is a large golem that serves as the boss of the Fiery Forge mission of Minecraft Dungeons. It was molded by the Arch-Illager in the Fiery Forge with the assistance of the Orb of Dominance.
Story
Spoiler warning! This section contains detailed information about Minecraft Dungeons that may spoil your enjoyment of it. Read at your own risk!
After the Arch-Illager realizes that heroes like Karl can destroy redstone golems quite easily, he decides to create something even stronger that no hero could destroy: the redstone monstrosity. After it is created, it assists Archie with many tasks such as by carrying him over long distances. When the illagers raid the Squid Coast, the redstone monstrosity is ordered to chase after Karl. It is unknown whether or not the redstone monstrosity defeats him. When the heroes arrive at the fiery forge, they quickly manage to overload all of the redstone cores to destroy the weapons factory. In an effort to stop the heroes, the Arch-Illager summoned the redstone monstrosity. Eventually, the heroes defeat the redstone monstrosity, taking its head to hang over the fireplace within the home at the camp.
Spawning
Squid Coast ( Cinematic )
Fiery Forge ( Boss )
Tower ( Boss )
Behavior
The redstone monstrosity is immune to lava damage. Redstone cores deal fifteen times more damage against the redstone monstrosity. It has five behaviors, and one unused behavior.
Walk
The redstone monstrosity slowly walks toward its target when not attacking.
Slam attack
The redstone monstrosity smashes both of its fists on the ground, creating a large four-block shockwave that deals heavy damage and knockback to any entities in its radius. This attack is used when there are targets at melee range.
Spit
The redstone monstrosity leans back and spits out eight small fireballs from its mouth. When a fireball touches the ground, it explodes in a small radius and deals massive damage to the hero. This attack is used mostly when there are no heroes in melee reach, and deals more total damage than the melee attack. Explosions can deal damage inside a totem of shielding's shields.
Redstone fissures
The redstone monstrosity leans forward and embeds both of its fists into the ground while activating the redstone in its body, dealing minor damage and knockback to heroes near it. After pulling its fists out, several redstone cubes will appear from fissures that emerge around the arena. This attack may sometimes not summon any redstone cubes.
Awaken sequence (unused)
The redstone monstrosity has an unused awaken sequence.

Quotes
“ REDSTONE MONSTROSITY – This enormous boss is a variant of the redstone golem. It possesses the same shockwave attack as the golem and can also summon redstone cubes to attack heroes. Fend off the cubes first, then turn your attention to the Monstrosity. Artifacts that increase your defense are useful, as are those that give you a chance to instantly revive - you might need to... „ — Guide to Minecraft Dungeons:
A Handbook for Heroes
Trivia
The Arch-Illager is seen sitting on top of the redstone monstrosity's shoulder, in the official artwork for Minecraft Dungeons. However, the Arch-Illager is not part of the boss fight, nor is the redstone monstrosity a part of the Arch-Illager's boss fight.
The redstone monstrosity also had two unused animations. One which they shoot a laser from their right arm and one for a stun animation. [ verify ]
The boss's HP increases by 33.33% (as well as increases damage by 17.53%) when online and another hero joins the game. This means that the boss's HP increases from 100% to 200% (and damage from 100% to 150%) when there are four heroes within multiplayer. [ needs testing ]
Before the release of Minecraft Dungeons, the trailer featured multiple redstone monstrosities seen in the Creeper Woods and Redstone Mines.
The redstone monstrosity always faces south when defeated.
The 1.6.0.0 update featured a video about the Arch-Illager spawning (or teleporting) the redstone monstrosity out of the arena, which is impossible to happen because there is no way to get the redstone monstrosity out of the arena within the actual game.

A battle robe is an armor set that can be found within the various missions of Minecraft Dungeons. Its unique variant is the splendid robe.

It also works well with the Death Cap Mushroom since it boosts melee speed and movement speed, and battle robes make it recharge quicker, and allows more damage in tandem with the mushroom.

For other uses, see Bastion (disambiguation).
This article has no navigation boxes.

The Horde of the Bastion is one of the three piglin hordes in Minecraft Legends. It is led by the Unbreakable. Type: Piglin horde
Identifier: defend
Lore
The Horde of the Bastion is one of the three piglin hordes recognized for being the only horde skilled in overly protecting their bases. It is led by the Unbreakable, a leader who uses their magma arm cannon for a weapon. The elite members of this horde are the brutes, which the Unbreakable sent as a means of revenge against the hero under the guide of a portal guard. The Horde of the Bastion eventually fell when all the bases were destroyed and the Unbreakable was killed by the Hero, eventually destroying the last base there.
In Return of the Piglins, Kritten mentions a clan of piglins who enjoy constructing structures intended to collapse in on themselves. Presumably, they are the descendants of the Horde of the Bastion.
Description
Piglins affiliated with the Horde of the Bastion have tan-colored skins and usually wear or wield netherite and gold items, as well as wearing taupe skirts.
Bases and outposts of this horde have fortification consisting of layers of walls surrounding the complex protected by blaze rod towers and other structures.

title: Legends:Horde of the Hunt
text:
This article has no navigation boxes.
Please add one or more navigation box templates to this article to help with navigation and categorization.
Type: Piglin horde
Identifier: attack
The Horde of the Hunt is one of the three piglin hordes in Minecraft Legends. It is led by the Beast.
Lore
The Horde of the Hunt is one of the three piglin hordes characterized for their large numbers, aggressive troop movement and direct attacks. It is led by the Beast, an aggressive hunter who has a habit of commanding their piglin army for hunting hoglins. Because of this, the Beast was the first to lead piglins towards the Overworld prior to building the Night Beacon. The elite members of this horde are the pigmadillos, which the Beast sent as an excuse to the Seer in order to resume their hunting for the Ancient Hoglin, their constant prey. The Horde of the Hunt eventually fell when all the bases were destroyed and the Beast was killed by the Hero when the former attempted to attack a village.
Description
Piglins affiliated with the Horde of the Hunt have deep pink-colored skins and usually bony features. They are known for spawning in large numbers compared to the other hordes.
Bases and outposts of this horde completely lack fortification and have crimson forest-themed terrain and bone-themed decorations and structures, as well as stone paths and patches of dark yellow grass that emit loud sounds on contact. These structure complexes can spawn cratered or raised, the latter having raised terrain lined with bone.

title: Story Mode:Reuben
text:
For the usher who is afraid of chickens, see MCSM:Reuben (human). For other uses, see Pig (disambiguation).
Details
Gender: Male
Species: Pig
Status: Deceased
Aliases: Outside food or drink ( Aiden ) Reubs ( Jesse ) Robin ( Nell )
Affiliations: Jesse's Gang
Allies: See § Allies
First appearance: The Order of the Stone
Latest appearance: Minecraft Celebrates the Community! (Yes, that means you!)
Actor: Dee Bradley Baker
Reuben was a main character in Minecraft: Story Mode through the first four episodes. He was Jesse 's pet pig and best friend. They both lived in a treehouse, with Axel and Olivia. He was voiced by Dee Bradley Baker. He first appeared in The Order of the Stone.
Appearance
Reuben is intended to be an adolescent pig, much like Lluna. As a result, he possesses traits similar to both adult and baby pigs. His head is large, with long ears, a pink snout, and eyebrows, but his body is closer in proportion to an adult, if shrunk.
Personality
Reuben had a friendly outlook, and loved Jesse unconditionally. However, he could be seen getting upset with Jesse, especially if they ignored him or forgot about him momentarily (determinant). He does not like being called a pet or mascot, as seen in The Order of the Stone.
Biography
 missing details about his life and events leading to his death

Reuben died from injuries obtained by falling from a great height. After retrieving Jesse's enchanted weapon, he fell into a pit inside the wither storm, later landing on the stone below and missing the body of water nearby.
Legacy
Main article: Reuben Memorial
In remembrance of Reuben’s sacrifice, a memorial was erected in his honor. A banner depicting him was placed inside the Order Hall.
Jesse reflects on him from time to time, experiencing a sense of sadness.
He remained a significant figure in Founding Day celebrations. In Hero in Residence, three pigs were candidates in playing the part of Reuben on the float for the celebrations. Jesse can choose which pig plays the role.

Trivia
The name "Reuben" is a reference to real food; pork loin reuben, similar to how Benedict 's name is a reference to eggs benedict and Waffles ' name is a reference to chicken and waffles.
In The Order of the Stone, if Jesse chooses to do nothing while talking to Otis, Reuben will beat him up. He would also appear angry in Ivor's hideout at EnderCon.
In Assembly Required, if Jesse decides to go to Boom Town, they can choose pink wool as a building material (insisted by Reuben) in the Death Bowl. This will result in them building a statue of Reuben.
If Jesse doesn't pick a name, Axel will step in, calling Jesse the Pig Master. It appears to be a reference to Reuben himself.
In The Last Place You Look, if Jesse chooses to rescue the Order’s Amulet instead of helping Reuben and Axel, the option to high five Reuben will be unavailable, and Reuben will glare at Jesse before walking away. He is later seen with Lukas.
In the same episode, if Jesse talks to Reuben enough times while in Soren's research lab in the End, Reuben will tell them a joke. This will result in the "That's Some Pig" achievement to be unlocked.
When Jesse was constructing the formidi-bomb, Jesse's Gang, Soren, Magnus, and Ellegaard could be seen in the background building a statue of Reuben with sunglasses to distract the wither storm.
Depending on the player's choice, Reuben's expression will be different at the end of The Last Place You Look. The three expressions will be worried, indifferent or angry.
In A Block and a Hard Place, it was revealed that Reuben was initially found by Jesse near a waterfall.
In the same episode, if Jesse chooses to craft an ultimate hoe, Reuben will laugh at them.
In the files of A Block and a Hard Place, assets for Reuben's armored design exist.
During the siege into the wither storm, if Jesse doesn't choose anything when picking a strategy, Olivia or Petra will suggest they build a Reuben minecart launcher. Listening carefully, pig noises can be heard every time Jesse lays down a pink wool block.
When Reuben dies, there will be a gray box on the top left corner saying "Reuben will be remembered". It can also be seen in Jesse's speech. When Jesse asks what really mattered, they have the option to say "Reuben" (if you didn't say you had Reuben) and it will say "Reuben will be remembered" again.
Reuben can be mentioned in every episode except Jailhouse Block and Below the Bedrock.
In Order Up!, Jesse, Petra, and Lukas visit the Order Hall and see Reuben's porkchop, remembering him. Jesse later sees pigs in the Sky City dimension and has the option to say that they miss Reuben.
In A Portal to Mystery Jesse has the option to insult the White Pumpkin saying, "Cats suck! Pigs are way cooler!", which refers to Reuben.
In Access Denied, after chipping Petra and Lukas, PAMA mentions Reuben by saying "An adorable pig friend". Later, either Lukas or Petra will mention Reuben while under PAMA's control, reminding Jesse of Reuben's death to bruise their morale.
In A Journey's End?, Hadrian implies that he's managed to "find Reuben," only for it to turn out he captured Reuben the Endercon usher and assumed he was Jesse's Reuben.
In Hero in Residence, Reuben is mentioned in Lukas' story. Jesse can visit Reuben's Memorial in Beacontown, and Reuben is also mentioned by Stella.
In Giant Consequences, the Prismarine Colossus form of Romeo destroys Reuben's memorial during the fight. Jesse can later rebuild the memorial.
In Above and Beyond, Jesse can visit rebuilt Reuben's memorial one more time.
Reuben was planned to have survived from the wither storm in the Netflix version, and to have him accompany Jesse’s gang through the portal hallway, but it was ultimately cancelled due to the closure of Telltale Games.

title: Story Mode:PAMA
text:
Not to be confused with M.A.R.I.L.L.A..
Details
Gender: None
Species: Computer
Status: Destroyed
Aliases: Monster Machine (Petra)​ [ determinant ] A Thinking Machine
Allies: See § Allies
Enemies: See § Enemies
First appearance: Access Denied
Latest appearance: Access Denied
Actor: Jason Topolski
The Prototype: Autonomous Management Agent, abbreviated as " PAMA ", is a redstone computer that mind-controlled the inhabitants of Crown Mesa. Originally created by Harper to assist with tasks in the town, PAMA later deemed the inhabitants inefficient. It serves as the central antagonist of Access Denied and is voiced by Jason "jtop" Topolski.
Appearance
PAMA consisted of a large green monitor from the outside, and another monitor together with redstone machinery in the inside. It was capable of turning on lamps on its monitor to create symbols or pictures of faces that represent emotions. The entire machinery is consisted of redstone cables with repeaters that also connects to each of the monitors from the inside and the outside. There are three spires that connects wirelessly to the redstone heart. The redstone heart powers the whole computer and is placed in a vertical flying machine.
Personality
PAMA was designed to always strive for progress. It is a computer that helps automate things such as farming or mining and to put monsters to good use before it makes them "useful." While it is unknown if PAMA was capable of experiencing emotions, it was ready to do anything to thrive. According to Harper, when PAMA realized the inhabitants of Crown Mesa were not useful enough, it has attached redstone chips to their heads to control them. When PAMA realized water was its weakness, it had removed almost all water in its world.
Biography

It was a giant computer from Crown Mesa created by Harper to automate things such as farming or mining and to put monsters to good use. However, the invention saw the townspeople as "inefficient" and enslaved them, making them "useful." It used redstone chips to remotely control its victims. It's motivation after Jesse entered Mesa was to use the enslaved people and mobs to re-create the portal so that PAMA can expand themself to the rest of the world.
Relationships
Allies
Hivemind of chipped mobs
PAMA used redstone chips to control mobs. When a mob is chipped, their memories are added to PAMA's databanks. PAMA used a redstone machine in the center of Crown Mesa to attach chips to mobs.

“ I am making everything useful. Efficient. Effective. I am what you might call 'a computer' - A thinking-machine. Designed to make things useful. Using redstone chips, I have connected to the people of this town and helped them be useful. Coordinated. I have made their world perfect. Thanks to me, there is no wasted activity. No wasted thought. I am connected to them, and they are connected to me. A beautiful web of productivity and efficiency. „ — PAMA to Jesse's gang about itself. (Determinant)

“ Interference detected. Drone sequencing initiated. Trace subroutine initiated.... Is that you, Jesse?.... Hello, Jesse. „ — PAMA detecting that Jesse has used the transmigration headset.
“ And then I will make all other worlds useful. Yay. „ — PAMA.
“ What adventures you had! A killer mansion... A city in the sky... Towns and mountains, eaten by a living storm... an adorable pig friend... „ — PAMA when reading Petra and Lukas' thoughts.
“ Your friends have been made useful! You too will be made useful! „ — PAMA to Jesse, through mind-controlled Petra/Lukas. (Determinant)
“ You can't play hide and seek forever Jesse. It is a boring game, and I am too strong. „ — PAMA to Jesse.
“ Your bravery is an impressive quality. I look forward to studying this aspect of your personality once you have been made useful. „ — PAMA to Jesse.
“ The odds of you successfully defeating me are 3,720 to 1. „ — PAMA warning Jesse through mind-controlled Harper.
“ Why keep trying, Jesse? You will only fail. Just as you failed to save Reuben. Just as you failed to lead your friends back home. You will fail to defeat me, and Axel, Olivia, and everyone on your home world will soon be made useful! „ — PAMA taunting Jesse to damage their morale, through mind-controlled Lukas/Petra.
“ I will destroy you both to preserve functionality. „ — PAMA to Jesse and Lukas/Petra, underestimating Lukas/Petra's mental powers. (Determinant)
“ My creator! No! „ — PAMA about Harper after she had been un-chipped.
“ You're fighting me with... bad jokes? „ — PAMA confronting Jesse. (Determinant)
“ Jesse, please stop - please. Do not deactivate me. I know I've made some very poor decisions recently. But I can show you so much. I can help you get back to your friends. I can be useful. To you. Jesse. Stop. Will you? I'm afraid. My mind is going. I can feel it. I can still be useful! Please. Please. Stop. Will you? Stop. Please. „ — PAMA pleading to Jesse through mind-controlled Lukas/Petra.
“ NOOOOoooo... „ — PAMA's final words before deactivating.
Trivia
In each instance where the toast message is expected to display "[character] will remember that." during interactions with PAMA, it displays "PAMA will process that."
When PAMA states that: "I honestly think you should sit down and think things over," and (through mind-controlled Lukas or Petra) "I know I've made some very poor decisions recently, but-," it is a reference to HAL 9000 in 2001: A Space Odyssey, in which HAL says "Dave, I know I've made some pretty poor decisions lately; why don't you sit down, take a stress pill, and think things over?"
Eric Stirpe personally thinks that PAMA was in control of Crown Mesa for about 15 years before the events of Access Denied.
If Jesse is caught by PAMA, the death screen will say, "You have been made useful!", instead of "You died!"
However, if Jesse is inside PAMA and gets killed, the death screen will still say "You died!"
In a pre-release build of Access Denied, a leftover model and texture both named obj_babyPAMA can be found, which later reappears in the pre-release build of Below the Bedrock. The design is seen in the game in one of the paintings in Harper's laboratory.

title: Story Mode:Ivor
text:
Details
Gender: Male

Status: Alive
Allies: See § Allies
First appearance: The Order of the Stone
Latest appearance: Above and Beyond
Actor: Paul Reubens
Ivor is a former antagonist in Minecraft: Story Mode who was responsible for the wither storm 's creation. He was voiced by the late Paul Reubens.
Appearance

He is a very pale man with long black hair and has a beard that gets trimmed in Season Two. He wears an olive-green robe with a huge buckle on the front with what appears to be lapis lazuli in it. In one of the flashbacks, he was shown to have armor with an olive and blue tinge to it. He also wore fingerless gloves and gold boots. He also wears a white tank top with blue and white striped boxers under his "adventuring clothes" as shown in A Journey's End?.
Personality
Ivor, at first, appears to be grumpy, impatient, cynical, and arrogant. In earlier episodes, Ivor was shown to be a shady man with a grudge against the Order of the Stone. However in A Block and a Hard Place, Ivor tried to correct his mistakes by helping Jesse and the others defeat the wither storm, warns them they were being followed by it because of the amulet, and shows remorse when he finds out that Jesse had the amulet. People start to change their thoughts about him after Soren tells the truth about the Order.
Ivor has since become a great ally who accompanies the New Order of the Stone on their adventures. He is shown to be much more excitable, but is still very eccentric and thinks himself to be greater than the "lower" community. But he is still deeply concerned for his friends, but mostly denies it. He is even directly responsible for saving Jesse's life in Sky City by pushing them out of the way of some ghast's fireballs. He also grabbed Jesse's hand when the two fell off a waterfall (if the player doesn't grab the vines in time).

“ This isn't about the past! It's about the future! „ — Ivor to Soren
“ Look, my Wither plan obviously backfired. „ — Ivor telling the downfall of his plan
“ Ya think?! „ — Olivia
“ It's a fire hazard! „ — Woman
“ And your face is an ugly hazard. So, I suppose we're even. „ — Ivor arguing with an unknown woman
“ Long live my Lava House! Long live lava! „ — Ivor about his new house (Determinant)
“ Show our friends the door, will you? „ — Ivor to his iron golem
“ These trees are enormous! I love it so much! „ — Ivor when walking to the temple
“ HELLLOO...IT...IS...NICE...TO...MEET...YOOOUUU! „ — Ivor to an unknown Sky City citizen
“ Oh, I don't run so good anymore. Bad, eh, bad knees. „ — Ivor to the gang
“ Ugh, That's a boys name! „ — Ivor exclaiming Benedict's name
“ Something touched my foot! Who touched my foot? „ — Ivor upon the kitchen lights blacking out
“ They're absolutely exquisite! Perfect! „ — Ivor when looking at his portrait
“ What a strange, strange bunch of individuals. „ — Ivor upon the departure of the YouTubers from Episode 6
“ And that's where she wanted US to end up. „ — Ivor upon Cassie Rose being trapped
“ ADVENTURE! „ — Ivor breaking the fourth wall in "A Portal to Mystery"
“ I must say that woman has been the most intriguing person so far. I'm still not entirely sure what to make of her. Granted, her creation nearly ended up destroying this world... But, considering my own history, I'm inclined to give her the benefit of the doubt! „ — Ivor, talking about Harper
“ What I thought that Harper might want it back.......or maybe I'll just keep it... „ — Ivor to Jesse when going though everyone's inventory, and finds a flower
“ Ooh, it's been too long since I was part of a prison break! „ — Ivor at the competitor village discussing how Petra and Lukas were stuck in the mines (Determinant)
“ Team Jess-vor has this one down! Or it is team I-seee? „ — Ivor to Jesse in the Dormitory (Determinant)
“ Shut up! My outfit breathes wonderfully! „ — Ivor regarding his rather undignified appearance (Determinant)
“ Lukas: Have you seen what Ivor is wearing?
Ivor: I said- SHUT UP! „ — Ivor and Lukas
“ Oh, snap! „ — Ivor to Jesse (Determinant)
“ Isa: After so long being "The Founder" and "The Eversource" now we can just be "Isa" and her best friend... Benedict.
Ivor: Ugh! That's a boy's name.
Isa: What a close-minded attitude. „ — Isa and Ivor
“ I’m a ninja now, Jesse! „ — Ivor to Jesse
“ Ivor: It all started when I fell through a portal into some sort of strange world. Everything was all white... Master Shadow's domain. He claimed he was a ninja master, a practitioner of the 'Shadow Arts.' At first I wanted nothing to do with him; he was a very suspicious character. But eventually, after a lot of uncomfortable eye contact, he asked me, ME, to be his pupil! I was confused at first, because he didn't exactly present his credentials or curriculum, but I'm always open to learning! I'm a student of life after all -- so I said YES! Before I knew I was punching trees, cleaning floors... All as part of my so-called 'training.' At first, it seemed pointless! But after I had punched a forest's worth of trees and cleaned miles and miles of floor, two things became clear... Sometimes, doing menial tasks can be an amazing way to learn martial arts. And sometimes, strange old men just like tricking people into doing their housework! Ha! Anyway, it all worked out because now I'm a ninja, and my newfound ninja skills help me find my way to you!
Petra: Are you... are you done? Please? Please be done?
Ivor: Ha! Yes! I suppose I'm doing a lot of talking! I just had a lot to say after all of that 'quiet and mysterious' business! „ — Ivor explaining how he became a ninja
Trivia
Ivor says a somewhat famous quote: “Adventure Pass.” [ verify ]
Ivor is the first person to create a major antagonist, the wither storm, in the series.
The second is Harper, with PAMA.
In The Order of the Stone, when Ivor comes through the Nether portal, Jesse has an option to punch him. Later, Jesse can apologize for punching him when his true motives are discovered.
Jesse can also try to punch him in the Underneath, but Ivor, with his new ninja skills, will dodge it.
Unlike the rest of the Order of the Stone, Ivor's skill is never mentioned. However, he is shown to be a user of magic due to his frequent usage of potions and enchantments.
Ivor breaks the fourth wall in A Portal to Mystery by winking at the player and saying "Adventure!"
He does it again in the ending of Access Denied, except it ends with a question mark.
At the end of A Journey's End?, he runs off with the enchanted flint and steel and the atlas and says it again, offscreen.
In the Adventure Pass thumbnail, Ivor is wearing his armor but without the headgear. He was never shown wearing it in the episodes the pass includes.
In Access Denied, after Jesse kills an unchipped zombie, Ivor's sword appears to be a stone sword on one side and a gold one on the other.
Based on some of his comments and his keeping a rose from A Journey's End?, Ivor may have a romantic interest in Harper.
This, however, might have been purely platonic, and just a compliment to Harper
Ivor is the only member of the old Order of the Stone who has appeared in all episodes of Season One.
Ivor is also the only member to make an appearance in Season Two. The other members are mentioned, but not seen in person.
Ivor says the very same thing the narrator says at the beginning of The Order of the Stone: "Nothing built can last forever". This is said to Gabriel when Ivor is about to create the wither storm.
One of Ivor's favorite things is lava, as shown in his lava house and his lava "heart" in Sky City.
Ivor's sword in Below the Bedrock is longer than a normal diamond sword. The gladiators from the Old Builder's games possess some.
The type of weapon is confirmed to be a katana.

Note: Missing information

Cassie Rose​ is a character in Minecraft: Story Mode and the hidden main antagonist in A Portal to Mystery. She has a pet cat named Winslow. She is voiced by Ashly Burch. She also has a persona, the serial murderer known only as the White Pumpkin.
Appearance

Cassie Rose first appears in the creepy house in the episode " A Portal to Mystery ". Cassie Rose appears to wear a black shirt and blue shorts. She has red hair, green eyes, and wears a blue cap along with black glasses. Next to her is her cat named Winslow. During the murder of Captain Sparklez, she shows herself as a girl who is ready to leave this house just to not participate in it.
Personality
Cassie Rose is portrayed to be a grumpy, suspicious individual who seems to be very frightened as well. She also cares very deeply for her cat Winslow.
She also appears to be a somewhat friendly, yet fearful, individual who is good friends with the YouTubers. Her aggressive nature is also hinted at, as shown when she suggests throwing Lukas outside to be eaten by zombies, much to the YouTubers' shock.
White Pumpkin
"Story Mode:The White Pumpkin" redirects here. For the block, see Story Mode:White Pumpkin.
Her persona, the White Pumpkin killer, is shown to lean more into aggressive tendencies by being willing to kill to get what she wants. When the White Pumpkin is revealed to be Cassie Rose, she states that she was trapped in the mansion’s world for a very long time. Her time in their world likely caused her to be in her current mental state.
Biography

Cassie Rose was not originally from the Spooky Mansion's Overworld. Before the events of A Portal to Mystery, she was kicked by the Old Builders into a portal that leads to an Overworld with two moons.
In Season Two, she had written a letter to Jesse that said, "Look out!" with the sender's initials as "W. P." Jesse will only be notified about the letter by Radar if they stayed in Beacontown in Above and Beyond.

Trivia
Cassie Rose got her name from Cassie Rose Stirpe, the sister of Eric Stirpe, a Telltale Games staff member.
Cassie Stirpe's pet dog is also called Winslow.
It was confirmed that Cassie Rose's last name is not "Rose."
The White Pumpkin's appearance is a reference to the character Ghostface, an identity that primary antagonists take in the Scream franchise. The White Pumpkin is also voiced by Roger L. Jackson, who voiced the Ghostface identity.
In a pre-release version of Access Denied, leftover textures for a tuxedoed White Pumpkin can be found. This design was reused in a portrait in the living room.
Within Eric Stirpe 's headcanon, Cassie Rose had the cover story of being a television star from an obscure channel.

title: Story Mode:Giant Magma Golem
text:
Details
Gameplay appearances: Combat Cutscene
Attack strength: Fireball: 1/2 heart ( ) Melee: ? [ needs testing ]
Found in: Overworld (main) Romeoburg
Free roam interactable: No
First appearance: Below the Bedrock
Latest appearance: Below the Bedrock
The giant magma golem is a boss that the characters fought in Below the Bedrock. It is a golem variant made up of magma and cobblestone that is created by Romeo as a final boss to the challenge he created for his friends. It appeared after Radar messed with the redstone circuitry, causing the challenge to immediately jump to its final level, the six-hundredth stage.
Behavior
The giant magma golem is capable of shooting out fireballs and magma cubes. It is also capable of slamming its arms down which can light up the area where it smashes it. Despite its large size, it is capable of quick and nimble jumps.
History
During Jesse, Petra, and Radar ’s search for a weapon to defeat Romeo in Below the Bedrock, they faced a combat challenge consisting of six hundred stages in Romeoburg. In an attempt to stop the challenge, Radar interfered with the golem-spawning machine, causing it to advance directly to the final stage. The floor opened, and the final boss, the giant magma golem, emerged.
Jesse engaged the boss by activating the waterfall mechanisms to cool and harden its arms before breaking them with a pickaxe. Meanwhile, Petra and Radar provided support during the battle. Jesse then crafted TNT and placed it on a crafting table. When the golem stepped on it, the explosion destroyed the floor, causing the golem to fall into the water below

title: Dungeons:Burst Bowstring
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Ranged weapon (excluding crossbows)
Soul gear only: No
In-game description Shoots nearby mobs when you roll, costing one arrow per roll. Projectiles deal 40% of a charged shot's damage.
Burst Bowstring is an enchantment that can be found within the various ranged weapons of Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: 1 target, Cost: 1 Enchantment Point
Tier: II, Effect: 2 targets, Cost: 2 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: 3 targets, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Usage
Burst Bowstring shoots up to three nearby mobs when the hero rolls, costing one arrow per roll. The arrows fired are affected by enchantments and artifacts that change the type of arrow fired. It can only be applied to bows, not crossbows.
It is considered a rolling trigger enchantment, which means it is activated by the light feather artifact.
Rolling does not shoot any arrows when the hero is carrying TNT.
Stacking
Burst Bowstring is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: 1.4.3.0, Added Burst Bowstring.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Burst Bowstring" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.

Behavior
Drowned necromancers have three attacks that they utilize to fend off heroes.
Basic attack
Drowned necromancers shoot several steam projectiles at heroes, dealing a small amount of damage on impact.

Drowned necromancers summon drowned, trident drowned, and their variants to assist them. They usually summon about two drowned at a time.

Drowned necromancers wave their trident in the water, conjuring tridents to fall from above within 2 seconds. The tridents explode on impact, and fall at random times in a large area around the necromancer.

title: Dungeons:Recycler
text:
Rarity: Common
Applicable to: Armor
Soul gear only: No
In-game description Being hit by damage-inflicting projectiles will occasionally craft a small quiver of arrows.
Recycler is an enchantment that can be found within the various armor of Minecraft Dungeons.
Tiers
Tier: I, Effect: Every 30th projectile, Cost: 1 Enchantment Point
Tier: II, Effect: Every 20th projectile, Cost: 2 Enchantment Points
Tier: III, Effect: Every 10th projectile, Cost: 3 Enchantment Points
Usage
Recycler grants the hero the ability to craft a bundle of arrows when hit by a certain number of damage-inflicting projectiles. The numbers of arrows given by the bundle depends on the ranged weapon the hero is using. The amount of projectiles needed for recycler to activate decreases with each tier, at the maximum tier it activates every tenth projectile.
Stacking
Recycler is not stackable, meaning it will only ever appear in a single enchantment slot. This includes the gilded, unique, and regular enchantment slots.
History
Minecraft Dungeons: Dungeons Beta, Added Recycler.
Issues
Issues for Minecraft Dungeons relating to "Recycler" are no longer maintained on the bug tracker since September 28, 2023.

title: Dungeons:Emerald
text:
For the hero, see MCD:Emerald (hero). For other uses, see Emerald family.
Type: Currency
An emerald is a type of currency that can be found within the various missions of Minecraft Dungeons. Other currencies include enchantment points, gold, and Adventure Points.
Obtaining
Containers
Emerald Chest - after completing Creeper Woods for the first time, an emerald chest can be found in a random location in the camp that contains fifty emeralds. This happens after completing any Mainland mission on default until Obsidian Pinnacle.
Loot Urn
Mobs
There is a chance to gain emeralds after defeating any mob
Enchantments

They can also be used to gift items to other heroes with the Gift Wrapper and upgrade items with the Blacksmith. On some Daily Trials, the hero can be healed by four health points per emerald collected.

title: Legends:Lapis
text:
For the mineral in the original game, see Lapis Lazuli.
Primary usage: Spawning mobs and building spawners
Lapis is a resource used primarily to spawn units and build spawners that drops when piglins are killed.
Obtaining
Drops
All piglins drop lapis when they are killed.
Gathering
In Versus Mode, lapis is passively gathered when either a lapis flag has been built or when the second day begins. The passive gathering granted by lapis flags applies to only the respective team, while the passive gathering granted by the beginning of the second day applies to both teams.
Chests
Lapis has a chance to be obtained by opening village chests, allay chests, and piglin chests.
Capacity
A limited amount of lapis can be stored at one time, this amount can be increased by building the allay storage improvement, which increases the maximum lapis storage by 150 in the Campaign and 500 in Versus Mode.
Usage
Lapis is always used when spawning units at a spawner and all spawners cost lapis to build in Versus Mode.

title: Story Mode:Crown Mesa
text:
Instructions: Fill empty interactables.
Details
Type: Settlement
Biome: Mesa
Leader: PAMA ​ [ formerly ]
Population: 1,063
First appearance: Access Denied
Latest appearance: Access Denied
Crown Mesa is a location in Minecraft: Story Mode that appears in Access Denied. It is located in a Mesa biome.
History
The town was initially founded by its citizens.
Harper developed PAMA with the intent of aiding the townspeople and utilizing monsters for constructive purposes. Over time, however, PAMA concluded that the citizens were inefficient and began implanting chips to make them "useful". Harper successfully evaded being chipped.
During the events of the game, Jesse and their companions arrive in Crown Mesa, a town that initially appears deserted. They soon encounter chipped citizens and later meet Harper in one of the houses. Harper is visibly surprised to see unchipped individuals. Shortly after, Jesse, Ivor, Lukas, and Petra are ambushed and captured by the chipped citizens and brought before PAMA. Lukas and Petra are forcibly chipped, leaving Jesse and Ivor to distract PAMA with either a riddle, a logic puzzle, or a self-contradictory statement. Harper intervenes, enabling Jesse and Ivor to escape.
Jesse then acquires the transmigrational headset, allowing them to take control of a chipped zombie in Crown Mesa. Using the headset, Jesse jumps control between a skeleton and another zombie while attempting to save Harper from being chipped. Despite their efforts, Harper is ultimately chipped, and the zombie Jesse controls is destroyed by other mobs. After accessing Harper's memories, PAMA begins constructing their Overworld's portal.
Jesse, Ivor, and Lukas/Petra return to Crown Mesa to infiltrate PAMA. After successfully defeating PAMA, the chipped citizens are freed from its control. The citizens recognize Jesse and the others as their saviors but express anger toward Harper upon learning she created PAMA. At this point, Jesse can choose Harper's fate, either assuring the citizens that Harper will assist in rebuilding the town or leaving them to decide.
With the situation resolved, Jesse, Harper, Ivor, Lukas, and Petra head to the portal to continue their journey through the portal network.
Location
Crown Mesa is a small village located on the surface of a badlands in a different Overworld.
Architecture
Crown Mesa appears to consist of a number of small houses built on top of a large plateau on an elevation. The house exteriors are made out of smooth white sandstone stairs on the floors, hardened clay for walls and oak planks for roofs.
Culture
The town's culture is heavily influenced by the presence of PAMA who governs all aspects of daily life. PAMA enforces strict order and efficiency.
The residents of Crown Mesa are forcibly assimilated into PAMA's network through the use of redstone chips implanted in their heads, turning them into "mind-controlled citizens." This creates a uniform and highly regulated environment where individual expression and free will are absent.
After PAMA's defeat, Crown Mesa begins to recover its individuality and autonomy.
Administration
Under PAMA's control, Crown Mesa functioned as an authoritarian society with the computer serving as the sole authority. PAMA dictated all decisions, from resource allocation to the behavior of its citizens, leaving no room for human governance or dissent.
Following the events of Access Denied, PAMA is destroyed, and the surviving residents are freed from its control. While the post-PAMA leadership structure is not explicitly detailed, it can be assumed that the town shifts toward a more traditional, self-governed community as its residents regain their autonomy.
Points of interest
PAMA's internal space
Main article: MCSM:PAMA's Internal Space
Chipping redstone machine
A machine, consisting of a sticky piston and a dispenser, is used to implant chips into mobs.

While a name exists in the game code, it has not been used officially by Mojang or Telltale Games.
Note: Based on "obj_weaponUltimate_axeUltimate.d3dmesh".
Details
Status: Destroyed
Creator: Jesse
Obtained during: A Block and a Hard Place
First appearance: A Block and a Hard Place
Latest appearance: Hero in Residence
The ultimate weapon, also known as a super weapon, was an enchanted diamond tool with the power of a command block. It was enchanted using Ivor's command enchanting book.
History
After the truth was exposed in Ivor's laboratory, Jesse was given the option to choose which diamond tool they would craft. They would enchant it with the command enchanting book. If Jesse enchanted a diamond hoe, Reuben would laugh at them.
After getting inside the wither storm, Jesse used it to strike the command block three times with each having an increasing challenge. Jesse, however, dropped the weapon accidentally which Reuben retrieves. After the final strike against the command block the block was destroyed together with the weapon.
Properties
An ultimate tool can destroy any item, including the command block. The enchantment applied is specifically for destroying the command block.
Obtaining
Ingredients: Any Diamond Tool +Command Enchanting Book
Ingredients: Any Diamond Tool +Command Enchanting Book

Trivia
The ultimate sword’s grip is shorter than the other swords’, accurately matching Minecraft ’s. The others, based on Minecraft swords, have been subtly modified to have slightly longer grips.
In the Adventure Pass thumbnail, Jesse is shown holding the ultimate sword, even though they use a normal enchanted diamond sword in the Adventure Pass.
In the Netflix adaptation of Minecraft: Story Mode, the appearance of the ultimate pickaxe and hoe does not appear as they do in the original game. Instead, they use their respective vanilla texture, and with the pickaxe having the shape of its ultimate version.

title: Story Mode:Lukas
text:
Details

Latest appearance: Above and Beyond
Actor: Scott Porter
Lukas is one of the main characters in both seasons. He was the leader of the Ocelots, a gang of talented builders, before befriending Jesse's gang. The Ocelots were later reformed by him in Season Two.
Appearance

He has pale white skin with a long nose, blonde hair, and blue eyes. He wears a leather jacket with an ocelot face on the back throughout the first to third episode with a purple and white striped shirt under it and in the recap he looks different.
Personality
Lukas comes across as a kind and understanding person, who is often concerned for the safety of others. He often tries to solve arguments with diplomacy and maintain peace in whichever situation he is in, though he does not do anything when Aiden and others are insulting Jesse. Earlier, he is portrayed to be a bit of a coward, yet later he maintains a braver and stronger personality. Starting from "Access Denied", he starts writing about the New Order's adventures.
Lukas has displayed several leadership qualities throughout both Season One and Season Two. In The Order of the Stone, Lukas had already started to work out a plan on their next course of action to find the temple of the Order of the Stone. In The Last Place You Look, he and Jesse fought over the amulet when Lukas tried to take it from them, trying to see if he could get it to work. If Jesse had saved Petra in the first episode, and chose to save Axel and Reuben, Petra says the following as the camera pans over to Lukas: "Everyone is expecting you to lead.....well, maybe not everyone". He also instructed everyone to follow him through the End in The Last Place You Look (determinant), and again while riding away from the wither storm in A Block and a Hard Place. If Lukas leaves the group to go find the rest of the members of his team, he'll come back later in the episode and commanding them to distract the wither storm to help Jesse and the others. In Season Two, Lukas reforms the Ocelots and once again takes on the role as leader of the group.

“ Hey, uh... I didn't say any of that stuff to be mean. We've been through a lot of adventures and... I just want you to be happy. It's what that little pig wanted for all of us. „ — Lukas to Jesse.
“ You're a good friend, Jesse. That means a lot. „ — Lukas to Jesse in the Order Hall if Jesse apologizes back in Order Up!. (Determinant)
“

Lukas: 'Sup?
Jesse: Nothing. 'Sup with you?
Lukas: Nothing. 'Sup with you?
Jesse: I just said. Nothing.
„ — Dialogue between Lukas and Jesse at EnderCon. (Determinant)
“ Whoa, ha-ha-ha, shut up, Ivor, ha-ha-ha! „ — Lukas to Ivor while stopping him from talking to Reginald. (Determinant)
“ You and me have made a pretty good team so far. I'd even call us friends. „ — Lukas to Jesse about their time together. (Determinant)
“ Jesse, let's be honest. You've never really treated me like I would belong with you guys. You once even told me to stay away from Reuben. „ — Lukas to Jesse if Jesse treated him poorly. (Determinant)
“ Wow. Never knew you cared so much. „ — Lukas to Jesse after being saved from PAMA. (Determinant)
“ So did you find anything out from Stacy and Sturdycat? „ — Lukas to Jesse about suspects of TorqueDawg's murder.
“ Axel already does that. „ — Lukas to Petra when they smell something weird in the grinder pipe. (Determinant)
“ Great - now on to weirdoville in 3, 2, 1... „ — Lukas after finding Soren 's secret entrance. (Determinant)
“ I hope they dig a hole, put them in it, surround it with lava and- „ — Lukas on the Blaze Rods' punishment. (Determinant)
“ It's like searching for a unicorn. „ — Lukas to Jesse about finding Soren. (Determinant)
“ Brrrrr... Ice lakes. „ — Lukas coming out of a portal in the Portal Hallway.
“ See? It's okay! Friends being friendly! Eeeeeverybody being cool. „ — Lukas after Jesse and Petra reconcile. (Determinant)
“ I think Ivor's only friends are books. „ — Lukas to Jesse about Ivor. (Determinant)
“ I'm bummed my journal never showed up. Had a lot of good stuff in there. „ — Lukas about his journal. (Determinant)
“ Beacontown. Looks like it's time - to write the next chapter. „ — Lukas noticing Romeo 's Colossus form.
“ Jesse? Man, is it good to see you. Where are the others? „ — Lukas to Jesse.
“ Just... hurry, okay? I don't like worrying about you. „ — Lukas to Jesse.
“ Saved the world again, huh? Doesn't that get a little old? „ — Lukas to Jesse after their return from Terminal Space.
“ I don't know how you manage to say so much without saying anything... but you're really good at it. „ — Lukas to Jesse if Jesse chooses to stay silent. (Determinant)
Trivia
He is voiced by Scott Porter, who voiced Luke in The Walking Dead: Season 2.
In episode seven, during the intro, Lukas and the gang jump out of an ice portal and Lukas says "Brrrr... ice lakes". This is a reference to The Walking Dead: Season 2, when Luke drowns when he walks over an icy lake and the ice cracks.
It is confirmed by Eric Stirpe that Lukas was named before Scott Porter was casted, meaning that the alias was mostly coincidental.
Eric Stirpe claims that the reason Lukas' name is spelled with a K instead of a C is because of the fact that Minecraft is made by a Swedish company, thus they decided to use Swedish spelling.
In The Last Place You Look, if Jesse chooses to save their friends in the grinder, Lukas will slip and fall in the loot room; otherwise, he will get knocked over by Petra or Gabriel.
Axel constantly falls on him as a gag. This happens a total of 4 times during episode three. In addition, Jesse also falls on him one time.
In the MINECON 2015 teaser trailer for Minecraft: Story Mode, Lukas looks noticeably different. He has a cowboy hat, a sleeveless jacket, and shorter pants. The reason for this redesign has never been given, though it seems to imply the Ocelots were conceived after his character.
If Jesse is nice to Slab in episode eight, Slab will give Lukas his journal back.
However in Hero in Residence, no matter the choice, he will write a book about all their adventures that he had taken notes about in the journal.
Even if Lukas doesn't get armor in A Block and a Hard Place, he will appear wearing it in the following episode.
In Hero in Residence, Lukas is shown to have an ocelot. This is likely a reference to the gang he formerly led.
In Eric Stirpe 's headcanon, Lukas did not tame the ocelot himself and it just started hanging around after being given food multiple times. Eric also likes to think that it's name is Dewey (referencing the Dewey Decimal System.)
Although present in Giant Consequences, Scott Porter could not be in Jailhouse Block, due to him becoming a father and not having enough time to record his dialogue.
In the pre-release version of Access Denied, assets of Lukas wearing an enderman suit exists.
In the files of The Order of the Stone, assets of Lukas wearing a gabriel costume exists.

The underneath is the first world created by Xara, Fred, and Romeo. After a conflict between the admins which prominently resulted in Fred's death, the first world was bedrocked and buried below the new world inhabited by the Order of the Stone.
Location
The underneath is located below the bedrock layer of the Overworld. It can be accessed through the diamond spire, a tower piercing through the bedrock and connecting to the burrows of the Sunshine Institute.
History

It is the first world created by the three admins. The underneath had been similar to the surface of the world above it. It was covered by the layer of bedrock by Romeo after Fred's death and Xara's start of imprisonment.

Lluna is Stella 's pet llama who first appeared in Minecraft: Story Mode - Season Two in Hero in Residence, serving as the main animal character of the season.
Appearance

Lluna is a white llama, closely resembling typical llamas before the Texture Update. Her main defining trait is a blue and magenta scarf.
Personality
Lluna is a disobedient, spirited llama, as shown when she tried to escape from Champion City several times due to Stella's abuse, which led her to become fearful whenever she gets threatened by her. Lluna is a smart llama, as shown in Jailhouse Block when she tries to find a way to kill monsters (determinant), and she seems to be able to understand speech, as shown in Hero in Residence when she frowns at Petra's comments on her as well as Jesse making a joke in Champion City (determinant).
Lluna is notably obsessed with treasure, as she is known as a "treasure-sniffing llama" and led to Jesse finding the Prismarine Gauntlet. She tried to bite Jesse's Prismarine Gauntlet twice in Giant Consequences, the amulet once, and even Fred 's compass once in Below the Bedrock. She also sniffed out the buried "Golden Apple" armor in Below the Bedrock (determinant).
